title: India and EU Conclude Free Trade Agreement Linking Climate Action and Commerce
summary:
India and the European Union have finalized a comprehensive free trade agreement eliminating tariffs on 90 percent of goods, expected to double bilateral trade from €124 billion to €248 billion within five years while generating €4 billion in annual savings. The agreement positions India as a strategic economic partner for the EU amid geopolitical tensions and provides India protection against potential U.S. tariff escalation under a second Trump administration. Both parties framed the deal as part of a deliberate effort to establish a stable, rules-based alternative to market volatility and policy fragmentation in global trade.

The FTA is structured to embed climate cooperation alongside commercial terms. Existing frameworks including the Clean Energy and Climate Partnership and the EU-India Trade and Technology Council coordinate work on renewables, energy efficiency, and green hydrogen development. India has signaled ambitions to become a hydrogen exporter to Europe, targeting $10 billion in foreign direct investment for 10 gigawatts of electrolyser capacity by 2030. The agreement also acknowledges the EU's Carbon Border Adjustment Mechanism, a carbon tariff on imports, with both sides discussing alignment of monitoring, reporting, and verification standards to manage potential trade friction while advancing climate objectives.

title: India and EU Conclude Free Trade Agreement With CBAM Flexibilities and Clean Tech Cooperation
summary:
India and the European Union have finalized a comprehensive Free Trade Agreement, announced by Prime Minister Narendra Modi and European Commission President Ursula von der Leyen at the 16th India–EU Summit. The accord addresses non-tariff barriers through regulatory cooperation and streamlined customs procedures, while incorporating provisions on the EU's Carbon Border Adjustment Mechanism that include technical cooperation, recognition of carbon pricing mechanisms, and financial support for emissions reduction. The agreement also establishes cooperation frameworks in artificial intelligence, clean technologies, and semiconductors.

The FTA represents a significant expansion of bilateral economic relations between two major global economic blocs that together account for 25 percent of global GDP and nearly one-third of global trade. More than 99 percent of Indian exports by value will receive preferential market access to the EU, with labor-intensive sectors including textiles, apparel, and engineering goods benefiting from tariff reductions up to 10 percent. The agreement encompasses ambitious commitments in services trade, a calibrated automobile liberalization package, and a mobility framework facilitating professional movement between the two regions.

title: Zambia And Switzerland Inaugurate Bilateral Climate Agreement Under Paris Article 6
summary:
Zambia and Switzerland formally launched a bilateral climate agreement on January 22 designed to advance greenhouse gas mitigation through voluntary international carbon market cooperation under Article 6.2 of the Paris Agreement. The agreement, signed in November at COP30, establishes a framework for transferring Internationally Transferred Mitigation Outcomes between the two countries, enabling Switzerland to offset emissions through certified carbon credits while channeling private finance into Zambian mitigation projects.

The accord represents Zambia's third bilateral Article 6 agreement in two years and positions the country as an emerging player in global carbon markets. The KliK Foundation, a Swiss entity mandated under the CO₂ Act, will finance and purchase carbon credits from verified Zambian projects that reduce emissions beyond national climate targets. These initiatives aim to address Zambia's energy sector investment gap exceeding $2 billion while generating employment, enhancing community resilience, and supporting renewable energy deployment, reforestation, and sustainable agricultural practices in a country vulnerable to climate-induced droughts.

title: Norway and Zambia Execute Article 6 Agreement for Emissions Reduction Payments
summary:
Norway and Zambia have finalized an agreement involving performance-based payments for emissions reductions connected to renewable electricity generation. The deal operates through the GGGI payments facility, establishing a mechanism to compensate verified emissions reduction outcomes.

This transaction represents implementation of Article 6 of the Paris Agreement, which enables international cooperation on emissions reductions through crediting mechanisms. The arrangement between the two nations demonstrates the use of financial instruments to support renewable energy development and climate mitigation activities in developing economies.

title: Vingroup Launches Carbon Credit Projects for Electric Motorcycles and Charging Stations
summary:
Vingroup, Vietnam's largest private conglomerate, announced two carbon credit projects focused on electric transportation infrastructure. The V-Green charging station project is registered under Verra's Verified Carbon Standard and is projected to reduce 3.5 million tons of CO2 annually by 2029, when approximately 6,000 charging stations with 620,000 ports will be operational. The VinFast electric motorcycle project follows the Gold Standard for the Global Goals certification. At current voluntary carbon credit market prices of $4 to $6 per ton, the charging station project alone could generate $14 to $21 million in revenue for the company.

These initiatives align with Vietnam's broader decarbonization strategy and represent Vingroup's entry into the voluntary carbon credits market. The projects are currently in mandatory stakeholder consultation phases before formal registration. VinFast reported selling 406,453 electric motorcycles in Vietnam during 2025, representing a 473 percent increase from the previous year and securing the top market position. Vietnam plans to establish a national carbon market by 2029 as part of its commitment to achieving net-zero emissions by 2050.

title: Vingroup Launches Carbon Credit Projects for Electric Vehicles and Charging Infrastructure
summary:
Vingroup initiated a stakeholder consultation on January 26 for two carbon credit projects focused on electric motorcycles and charging stations. The charging infrastructure project alone is projected to reduce up to 3.5 million tonnes of CO₂ emissions annually, with Vingroup targeting approximately 6,000 charging stations and 620,000 ports by 2029. The motorcycle project operates under Gold Standard's GS4GG framework, while the charging station credits fall under Verra's Verified Carbon Standard, both internationally recognized verification schemes.
 
 The projects represent growing private-sector engagement in voluntary carbon markets within Vietnam. At current voluntary carbon credit prices of $4-6 per credit, Vingroup could generate $14-21 million in annual revenue from credit sales. Credits approved for the Carbon Offsetting and Reduction Scheme for International Aviation could trade at significantly higher prices around $20 each, with Verra's VCS standard confirmed as eligible for CORSIA compliance from 2027 to 2029. The electric motorcycle initiative is expected to run for five years with possible extensions, while similar smaller-scale projects by other companies have also registered under recognized standards.

title: India Releases Decarbonization Roadmaps For Cement And Aluminium Sectors
summary:
Niti Aayog has unveiled decarbonization roadmaps for India's cement and aluminium sectors as the nation pursues its net-zero emission target by 2070. The cement sector aims to reduce carbon intensity from 0.63 to 0.09-0.13 tonnes of CO2 equivalent per tonne by 2070, while aluminium production is projected to increase sevenfold and tenfold respectively. Both roadmaps propose strategies including renewable energy adoption, fuel switching, carbon capture and storage deployment, and expanded carbon credit trading mechanisms.

These initiatives address mounting pressure from the European Union's Carbon Border Adjustment Mechanism, which took effect January 1, 2026, and imposes carbon tariffs on imports including cement and aluminium. Without decarbonization efforts, Indian exporters face potential price reductions of 15-22% to maintain competitiveness under the new trade rules. The timing coincides with India's planned Free Trade Agreement announcement with the EU, positioning decarbonization as critical for preserving export competitiveness and economic stability, particularly for smaller enterprises facing higher compliance costs.

title: India Establishes Carbon Credit Trading Scheme Framework For Emissions Reduction
summary:
India has advanced its carbon market infrastructure through the adoption of the Carbon Credit Trading Scheme in June 2023, which operates through dual mechanisms combining mandatory compliance and voluntary offset components. The compliance mechanism targets energy-intensive sectors including cement, iron and steel through an emission intensity-based system, where entities earning Carbon Credit Certificates can trade them on power exchanges while those exceeding targets must purchase equivalent credits. In 2025, the government notified greenhouse gas emission intensity targets for four additional sectors: aluminium, cement, Chlor-Alkali, and Pulp and Paper. The framework builds on existing infrastructure from the Perform, Achieve and Trade scheme while gradually transitioning toward a fully operational compliance carbon market.
 
 The voluntary offset mechanism allows non-obligated entities to register projects that reduce, remove, or avoid greenhouse gas emissions and earn tradable credits. The government has approved offset activities across ten sectors including energy, agriculture, waste management, forestry, transport, and carbon capture and storage. This dual-track approach reflects international carbon market design principles, with the Economic Survey noting that major economies including the European Union, Korea, and China have implemented comparable emissions trading systems demonstrating various implementation models and stages of market development.

title: LanzaTech Awarded Contract To Build Ethanol Facility In India Using Agricultural Waste
summary:
LanzaTech Global has secured a contract from Spray Engineering Devices Ltd to construct a next-generation ethanol production facility in Uttar Pradesh, India, utilizing sugarcane bagasse as feedstock. The facility will process up to 300 tons of bagasse daily and produce sustainable fuels, chemicals, and biochar while operating under India's PM JI-VAN Yojana government program supporting advanced bioethanol production from agricultural waste.

The project represents part of the SED Smart Village framework, which aims to capture economic value from renewable energy and carbon resources by converting low-cost power into high-value products including aviation fuels and e-fuels. LanzaTech's bioreactor technology uses proprietary microbes to convert carbon-rich gases and green hydrogen into ethanol, diverting agricultural waste from burning while supporting circular economy principles and rural economic development in sugarcane-growing regions.

title: LanzaTech Wins Contract to Build Bio-Ethanol Plant in Uttar Pradesh
summary:
LanzaTech Global has been selected by Spray Engineering Devices Ltd to construct a carbon recycling facility in Uttar Pradesh that will convert sugarcane bagasse into ethanol and other fuels and chemicals. The project, part of SED's Smart Village initiative, will include a 24,000 tonnes per annum ethanol plant capable of processing up to 300 tonnes of bagasse daily and is expected to achieve approximately 19,200 tonnes of CO₂ equivalent annual abatement. The facility will represent the first in India to employ LanzaTech's technology for this specific application and is anticipated to begin operations within two years.

The plant will utilize syngas generated through oxygen-enriched air gasification of sugarcane bagasse, with LanzaTech's proprietary microbes converting carbon-rich gases into ethanol for use in sustainable aviation fuel and renewable diesel production. The project aligns with India's PM JI-VAN Yojana government programme supporting advanced bioethanol production and aims to reduce agricultural residue burning while generating nutrient-rich biochar for soil improvement. This development expands LanzaTech's operational presence in India, complementing its existing commercial-scale plant at Indian Oil Corporation's Panipat refinery.

title: EcoCeres Opens Malaysia's First Commercial Sustainable Aviation Fuel Plant
summary:
EcoCeres officially launched Malaysia's first commercial-scale sustainable aviation fuel production facility in Johor's Tanjung Langsat industrial area on January 26, with an annual production capacity of 420,000 tonnes. The facility, backed by Bain Capital, began commercial operations in October and represents a significant milestone for the country's renewable fuels sector and aviation decarbonization efforts.

The plant positions Malaysia as a regional hub for sustainable aviation fuel production, leveraging access to feedstock from South-east Asia and established seaborne logistics networks. EcoCeres, now the world's second-largest sustainable aviation fuel producer, targets Europe as its primary market due to regulatory blending mandates, while simultaneously developing demand in Asia-Pacific markets including South-east Asia, Australia, New Zealand, and Singapore.

title: Multiple Renewable Fuel and Clean Energy Projects Advance Globally
summary: Several developments in the renewable and clean energy sectors occurred in January 2026. CVR Energy completed the reversion of its Wynnewood refinery's renewable diesel unit back to hydrocarbon processing in December while retaining future renewable production capability. The U.K. Department of Transport announced £43 million in funding for green aviation projects including clean fuels and zero-emission aircraft. LanzaTech Global closed a $20 million funding round from investors including new backer SiteGround for its carbon recycling operations. EcoCeres officially opened Malaysia's first sustainable aviation fuel production facility in Pasir Gudang, Johor. The USDA's Rural Business Cooperative Service implemented a 90-day pause on loan guarantee processing for biodigester projects.

title: Global Carbon Credit Market Projected To Reach 7.1 Trillion By 2034
summary:
InsightAce Analytic released a comprehensive market assessment report projecting significant expansion of the global carbon credit market. The market was valued at USD 531.2 billion in 2024 and is anticipated to grow to USD 7126.6 billion by 2034, representing a compound annual growth rate of 29.8% over the 2025-2034 forecast period. The analysis covers both voluntary and compliance market segments, including various project types such as avoidance, reduction, and nature-based or technology-based removal initiatives across multiple sectors including aviation, energy, building, power, industrial, and transportation applications.

The report examines market drivers including increased institutional investment from banks, venture capital firms, and alternative financing entities seeking opportunities in the carbon credit ecosystem. The analysis addresses carbon credits as verified, quantifiable emission reductions generated through defined projects adhering to established standards with documented ownership and rigorous monitoring processes. Notable market participants include organizations such as 3 Degrees, Carbon Credit Capital, Climate Impact Partners, EcoAct, and South Pole Group, alongside international institutions like the World Bank's Climate Change Fund Management Unit established to support climate-resilient development and low-carbon technology advancement.

title: Carbon Offset And Credit Industry Projected To Reach USD 20 Trillion By 2035
summary:
The carbon offset and carbon credit industry is expected to grow from USD 1.25 trillion in 2025 to USD 20.06 trillion by 2035, representing a compound annual growth rate of 32.02 percent. The market functions as a key mechanism for addressing global greenhouse gas emissions through instruments that quantify reductions or removals of carbon dioxide and other gases, generated via projects such as renewable energy deployment, reforestation, methane capture, and energy efficiency improvements.
 
 Multiple interconnected factors are driving market expansion, including increasing regulatory pressure from governments and international climate agreements, corporate net-zero commitments, rising consumer and investor awareness of climate impacts, expansion of emissions trading systems across regions, and growth in climate finance mechanisms. Technological advances in digital monitoring, satellite imagery, remote sensing, data analytics, artificial intelligence, and blockchain are enhancing transparency, credibility, and scalability of the market by improving verification accuracy, reducing fraud risks, and addressing historical concerns around double counting and standardization challenges.

title: Joint Venture Launches Digital Infrastructure Supporting Article 6 Paris Agreement Carbon Markets
summary:
A new partnership among technology firms Aleria, Tawasal, and Xange.com has been established to develop digital systems supporting carbon mitigation efforts under Article 6 of the Paris Agreement. The joint venture aims to create a carbon mitigation pipeline valued at over US$100 billion, focusing on forest and nature-based internationally transferred mitigation outcomes across Africa, South America, the Middle East, and Asia. The collaboration will build digital monitoring, reporting, and verification systems alongside a global infrastructure platform called GEMIS to facilitate emissions tracking and verification.

The joint venture addresses a critical need for robust digital infrastructure to implement Article 6 mechanisms, which enable countries to cooperate on emissions reductions and transfer mitigation outcomes across borders toward their climate targets. The partnership will establish a central registry and settlement platform with a financial custodian bank in Chicago to oversee transfers, integrating with Tawasal's digital platform to connect millions of users and support transactions across regions. This infrastructure aims to ensure compliance with Paris Agreement requirements and prevent double-counting errors in international carbon market cooperation.

title: UN Launches Digital Infrastructure to Operationalize Article 6 Paris Agreement
summary:
The United Nations Framework Convention on Climate Change has awarded a multi-year contract to Trovio to build digital infrastructure supporting Article 6 of the Paris Agreement. The infrastructure includes registries for Article 6.2 International Transferred Mitigation Outcomes and Article 6.4 credits, an interoperability hub to align national and international systems, and a minimum viable product for pilot testing. The project was procured by the United Nations Development Programme in collaboration with the UNFCCC Secretariat.

This infrastructure development represents the transition from policy design to operational delivery of international carbon market mechanisms. The digital systems are intended to establish the foundational architecture for cross-border carbon credit transfers, enabling transparency, integrity, and secure cooperation as countries scale their climate mitigation efforts.

title: US Biomass-Based Diesel Market Expects Record Growth In 2026 Amid Policy Uncertainty
summary:
The US biomass-based diesel industry is experiencing optimistic sentiment following the Clean Fuels Conference, with expectations for approximately 2 billion additional gallons of mandated volumes under the Renewable Fuel Standard in 2026, representing the largest year-over-year increase in the program's history. However, this growth trajectory depends on finalization of key policies, including RFS2 rules and Section 45Z carbon-adjusted tax credits, which are expected by end of Q1 2026 but have already faced delays. Many biodiesel plants remained idled in 2025 due to uncertainty around tax incentives that could provide up to $1 per gallon in support.

The biomass-based diesel sector operates as a policy-driven market where federal mandates and tax incentives are essential to financial viability. A significant market shift is occurring in feedstock sourcing, with the carbon-centric 45Z framework and RFS priorities now favoring domestic production over imports such as used cooking oil and animal fats. Import volumes have declined to near zero in 2025, creating pressure on domestic feedstock supplies and highlighting the need to develop scalable, lower-carbon input sources to sustain projected production growth.

title: Renewable Diesel Market Projected To Reach 48.33 Billion Dollars By 2032
summary:
The global renewable diesel market reached USD 24.67 billion in 2024 and is forecast to expand to USD 48.33 billion by 2032, growing at a compound annual growth rate of 8.77 percent. North America leads market expansion with a 46.5 percent share, driven by stricter emission regulations, decarbonization initiatives, and increased waste-based feedstock availability across transportation, power generation, and industrial applications.
 
 Recent regulatory developments and corporate activity have accelerated market growth. The U.S. Environmental Protection Agency established record biomass-based diesel volume obligations, while clarified tax credit guidance provided production incentives. Key industry players including Phillips 66, Marathon Petroleum, and ENEOS have expanded or launched renewable diesel production facilities, with strategic acquisitions strengthening feedstock supply chains and production capacity throughout North America and Asia Pacific regions.

title: SAF One Energy Forms Strategic Partnership To Scale Sustainable Aviation Fuel Production
summary:
SAF One Energy Management Limited announced a strategic alliance with Honeywell UOP and Tata Projects Limited to expand sustainable aviation fuel production capacity globally, with particular focus on India. The partnership leverages Honeywell UOP's Ecofining process technology, which converts waste materials such as used cooking oil and animal fats into renewable diesel and sustainable aviation fuel, while Tata Projects serves as the integrated engineering, procurement, and construction partner for facility development.

The collaboration supports SAF One's rapid deployment strategy amid strong regulatory and market drivers for SAF adoption. Industry projections indicate the SAF market could exceed $25 billion by 2030, with expected compound annual growth of 65.5 percent. SAF One's recent acquisition of Green Biotrade enhances feedstock supply chain management, while regulatory mandates including the European Union's ReFuelEU Aviation Regulation and corporate sustainability commitments continue to accelerate demand for alternative aviation fuels.

title: Honeywell, Tata Projects, SAF+ One Partner To Scale Sustainable Aviation Fuel Production
summary:
Honeywell, Tata Projects, and SAF+ One announced a strategic partnership to expand sustainable aviation fuel (SAF) production, beginning in India and extending to additional regions. The collaboration leverages Honeywell's proprietary technology that converts renewable biomass and waste materials into high-quality fuel compatible with existing aircraft infrastructure without mechanical modifications. Tata Projects contributes large-scale infrastructure development expertise while SAF+ One focuses on sustainable production pathways and feedstock sourcing.

The partnership addresses the aviation industry's need to reduce carbon emissions and comply with international environmental regulations. As global air travel demand increases, SAF is positioned as the primary decarbonization solution for commercial aviation. The collaboration aims to transition SAF from a niche alternative to a mainstream commodity, combining advanced technology with large-scale infrastructure capabilities to make aviation's future economically viable and environmentally responsible.

title: Bio-Based Sustainable Aviation Fuel Market Projected to Reach $4.77 Billion by 2032
summary:
The global bio-based sustainable aviation fuel market is experiencing rapid expansion, growing from $420.04 million in 2024 to an anticipated $4.77 billion by 2032 at a compound annual growth rate of 31.0%. Major industry players including Gevo, Neste, World Energy, SkyNRG, and Indian Oil are driving this growth through facility expansions, production partnerships, and technology deployments across North America, Asia-Pacific, and Europe. Key developments include alcohol-to-jet production facilities, municipal waste conversion technologies, and HEFA-based processing methods that improve fuel yield and sustainability metrics.

Regional initiatives are accelerating market adoption, with the United States leading through major carrier partnerships and established SAF supply chains, Japan advancing through government subsidies and airline collaborations, and Asia-Pacific expanding through commercial-scale operations in Singapore, Australia, and India. The sector is consolidating through strategic mergers and acquisitions, including technology acquisitions by Gevo, regional expansions by SkyNRG, and portfolio strengthening by Indian Oil. Technological partnerships between major energy companies and SAF specialists are establishing integrated supply ecosystems to meet growing airline blending mandates and carbon neutrality aviation targets.

title: Keppel and Aster to Assess Jurong Island Sustainable Aviation Fuel Plant
summary:
Keppel's infrastructure division has partnered with Chandra Asri's joint venture Aster to evaluate the development of a commercial-scale sustainable aviation fuel facility on Jurong Island in Singapore. The proposed plant would convert low-carbon ethanol into jet fuel with an annual production capacity of up to 100,000 tonnes. The companies will conduct front-end engineering design studies to assess technical specifications, capital requirements, and financing structures needed to proceed with a final investment decision, building on a completed feasibility study that validated the project's technical and commercial viability.

The facility would represent one of Asia's first purpose-built ethanol-to-jet sustainable aviation fuel plants and is designed to support Singapore's national sustainable aviation fuel targets while meeting growing regional airline demand. The Jurong Island location provides advantages through access to existing industrial infrastructure and logistics networks. The partnership combines Aster's refining and chemical expertise with Keppel's experience in developing and operating complex energy and environmental assets, with the project expected to offer flexibility in sourcing bioethanol feedstocks as regional markets evolve.

title: Global Sustainable Aviation Fuel Market Projected to Reach USD 39.28 Billion by 2035
summary:
The global sustainable aviation fuel market is projected to experience substantial growth, expanding from USD 1.45 billion in 2024 to approximately USD 39.28 billion by 2035, representing a compound annual growth rate of 35.0%. This expansion is driven by regulatory mandates including CORSIA and ReFuelEU Aviation requirements, airline sustainability commitments through long-term offtake agreements, declining production costs, and technological improvements in SAF production processes. Sustainable aviation fuel, produced from renewable or waste-based feedstocks such as used cooking oil, agricultural residues, and synthetic pathways, can reduce lifecycle greenhouse gas emissions by up to 80% compared to conventional jet fuel.

The aviation industry is increasingly recognizing SAF as the primary near- and medium-term solution for decarbonizing existing aircraft fleets to meet net-zero emission targets by 2050. Airlines, airports, aircraft manufacturers, and fuel producers are collaborating to secure SAF supply chains and establish production capacity through multi-billion-dollar investments. Ongoing technological innovations in catalytic processes and power-to-liquid technologies are further reducing production costs and expanding viable feedstock options, positioning SAF as a central pillar of aviation's energy transition.

title: Indian Oil Corporation and Akasa Air Explore Sustainable Aviation Fuel Supply Partnership
summary:
Indian Oil Corporation Limited and Akasa Air signed a Letter of Intent at Wings India 2026 to investigate the supply of Sustainable Aviation Fuel. The preliminary agreement establishes a collaborative framework to assess potential SAF volumes, delivery logistics, timelines, and approved feedstocks and production methods to support Akasa Air's sustainability objectives.
 
 The partnership reflects broader industry momentum in India's aviation decarbonization efforts, aligned with the nation's net-zero 2070 target and a planned 1% SAF mandate for international flights by 2027. India's aviation fuel market is projected to reach USD 153.25 million by 2030, with SAF identified as critical to achieving 53-66% of sectoral net-zero goals. Indian Oil has established multiple SAF collaborations, including agreements with Air India and LanzaJet, while competitors like BPCL and Reliance Industries are pursuing parallel initiatives.

title: Asia Emerges as Major Sustainable Aviation Fuel Market With New Mandates
summary: Asian governments are implementing regulatory requirements to increase sustainable aviation fuel (SAF) adoption, positioning the region as a significant growth frontier for the industry. Singapore mandated that SAF comprise 1% of jet fuel at its airports by 2026, with plans to reach 5% by 2030. South Korea requires SAF on international flights starting in 2027, targeting 7-10% adoption by 2035, while Thailand is developing national SAF standards. These policy commitments follow a similar pattern seen in Europe, where early adopters prompted neighboring countries to establish their own requirements. The Neste facility in Singapore currently produces one million tonnes of SAF annually by converting organic waste products like used cooking oil and animal fats into jet fuel, with most output exported to Australia and Europe. Demand for SAF in Southeast Asia is projected to increase substantially, from 15,000 barrels per day in 2030 to over 700,000 barrels daily by 2050, alongside corresponding production expansion. Malaysia recently opened its first commercial-scale SAF facility with annual capacity of 420,000 metric tonnes, reflecting growing regional investment in sustainable aviation fuel infrastructure.

title: India Expands Carbon Credit Trading Scheme to Cover 490 Industrial Entities
summary:
India's Union government extended its Greenhouse Gas Emission Intensity (GEI) regulatory framework in January 2026 by bringing 208 additional industrial entities under mandatory compliance through the Carbon Credit Trading Scheme (CCTS). This expansion raises the total number of regulated participants in the Indian Carbon Market to 490, significantly broadening the scope of sectors required to meet emissions reduction targets while maintaining production growth.

The GEI approach measures emissions relative to industrial output rather than in absolute terms, allowing companies to reduce pollution intensity per unit produced without restricting overall production volumes. Under the scheme, firms exceeding their assigned reduction targets generate tradeable carbon credits, while those falling short must purchase credits to achieve compliance. The Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change oversees target-setting, monitoring, and enforcement, creating a market-driven system that incentivizes investment in cleaner technologies and operational efficiency across India's most emission-intensive industries.

title: India Expands Carbon Credit Trading Scheme With New Industrial Emission Targets
summary:
India's Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change notified amended Greenhouse Gas Emission Intensity Target Rules on January 13, 2026, expanding the mandatory scope of the Carbon Credit Trading Scheme to include four additional energy-intensive sectors: secondary aluminium, petroleum refineries, petrochemicals, and textiles. The amendment establishes emission intensity targets for obligated entities using 2023–24 as the baseline, with a phased compliance approach beginning in 2025–26 and continuing through 2026–27. Petroleum refineries will operate under a Normalized Refinery Generation Factor that adjusts targets based on facility complexity, and major industrial operators including Bharat Petroleum Corporation Limited, Indian Oil Corporation, Reliance Industries, and textile manufacturers have been designated as obligated entities.

The regulatory expansion aims to deepen industrial participation in emissions control while establishing a functional domestic carbon market mechanism. Companies exceeding their emission intensity reduction targets can earn and trade carbon credits, while underperformers must purchase credits or face penalties. The framework links emission reductions to production efficiency rather than absolute output, enabling industrial growth alongside environmental impact reduction and supporting India's progression toward net-zero emissions goals.

title: India Energy Week Examines International and Domestic Carbon Market Architectures
summary:
Carbon markets are shifting toward policy-anchored mechanisms that support national climate commitments and industrial decarbonization. The Paris Agreement's Article 6 establishes structured pathways for international cooperation through bilateral credit transfers and a UN-supervised mechanism, while India has operationalized its domestic Carbon Credit Trading Scheme to price greenhouse gas emissions and incentivize compliance and over-compliance through tradable carbon credit certificates. An India Energy Week focus session will explore how international frameworks under Article 6 and India's domestic carbon market architecture interact and evolve to mobilize climate finance while maintaining environmental integrity.

India's Carbon Credit Trading Scheme operates through two mechanisms: a compliance pathway requiring obligated entities to meet emission intensity reduction targets, and an offset pathway allowing non-obligated entities to earn credits through eligible projects. These parallel structures at the international and domestic levels create opportunities for Indian industry, policymakers, and investors to participate in carbon markets aligned with India's enhanced Nationally Determined Contributions under the Paris Agreement.

title: Global Carbon Markets Project Supports Nations Implementing Climate Instruments
summary:
The Global Carbon Market project provides technical assistance to public and private sector stakeholders across Eastern Africa, the Caribbean, and Southeast Asia in establishing and deploying market-based climate mitigation tools. The initiative conducts research, delivers capacity-building programs, and implements pilot initiatives to support countries in achieving their Nationally Determined Contributions and Long-Term Strategies while developing cooperative approaches under Article 6 of the Paris Agreement.

The project facilitates knowledge transfer and strategic guidance on carbon market mechanisms, trains government negotiators for climate conferences, and fosters regional cooperation on emissions trading. By bridging public authorities and private enterprises, the initiative aims to advance understanding of how market-based instruments can effectively contribute to national and international climate objectives.

title: India Integrates Climate Action With Development and Energy Security Strategy
summary:
India's Economic Survey 2025–26 outlines a development-centered climate strategy that combines adaptation, mitigation, and behavioral change within its growth framework. The government emphasizes that development itself serves as climate adaptation for vulnerable economies, with adaptation spending rising from 3.7 percent of GDP in FY16 to 5.6 percent in FY22. Key initiatives include the National Action Plan on Climate Change with nine missions, State Action Plans translating priorities locally, and urban planning incorporating climate risk considerations.
 
 The strategy addresses energy transition through expanding non-fossil fuel capacity, which reached 51.93 percent of installed power as of December 2025. India pursues mitigation through renewable energy, green hydrogen, nuclear power, and energy efficiency while acknowledging challenges in material availability and storage. The government is securing critical minerals through the National Critical Mineral Mission and international partnerships, enacted the SHANTI Act to enable private nuclear sector participation, and implemented the Carbon Credit Trading Scheme combining compliance and voluntary offset mechanisms. Mission LiFE promotes behavioral change as a foundation for climate policy implementation.

title: CF Industries and POET Launch Low-Carbon Fertilizer and Ethanol Supply Chain Pilot
summary:
CF Industries and POET announced a collaborative pilot project with agricultural cooperatives to develop a low-carbon fertilizer supply chain that reduces the carbon intensity of corn production and ethanol manufacturing. The initiative involves tracking low-carbon ammonia fertilizer from CF Industries' Donaldsonville Complex through distribution networks operated by WinField United and agricultural cooperatives across Iowa, Minnesota, Missouri, and Nebraska, with corn subsequently processed at POET ethanol facilities. The first distribution and application of low-carbon ammonia was completed in fall 2025, with projections indicating the corn produced could generate 5-6 million gallons of ethanol with reduced carbon intensity.

CF Industries captures and permanently stores CO₂ emissions during ammonia production at its facility, which operates at capacity to produce 1.9 million tons of low-carbon ammonia annually. The pilot demonstrates a vertically integrated approach connecting low-carbon fertilizer manufacturing to retail distribution, farmer application, and bioethanol production, creating a traceable value chain for certified low-carbon inputs. Participants emphasized the initiative's dual focus on environmental stewardship and economic benefits for rural agricultural communities and farmers in the Midwest.

title: CF Industries and POET Partner to Pilot Low-Carbon Fertilizer Supply Chain
summary:
CF Industries, POET, and major agricultural cooperatives including WinField United, NuWay-K&H, New Cooperative, and Farmer's Cooperative have launched a collaborative pilot program to develop and implement a low-carbon fertilizer supply chain across the Midwest. The initiative focuses on producing low-carbon ammonia fertilizers at CF Industries' Donaldsonville Complex—where carbon emissions are captured and permanently stored—and distributing them through cooperative retail channels to corn growers in Iowa, Minnesota, Missouri, and Nebraska. The corn cultivated with these lower-carbon fertilizers will then be processed by POET's ethanol plants to produce approximately 5 to 6 million gallons of ethanol with reduced carbon intensity.

The pilot represents an integrated approach to decarbonization across the agricultural value chain, connecting fertilizer manufacturing, distribution, farm application, and renewable fuel production. Participants aim to demonstrate that meaningful emissions reductions can be achieved at scale without disrupting agricultural productivity or rural economies. The consortium completed its first distribution and field application cycle during the fall 2025 growing season, with CF Industries' low-carbon ammonia facility capable of producing up to 1.9 million tons annually at full capacity.

title: TotalEnergies Reduces Clean Fuel Investments Amid EU SAF Mandate Concerns
summary:
TotalEnergies CEO Patrick Pouyanné announced at the World Economic Forum that the company will scale back investments in sustainable aviation fuel and other clean fuels, citing expectations that the European Union will weaken its ReFuelEU mandates. Pouyanné criticized the proposed SAF target trajectory from 2% in 2025 to 6% in 2030 as economically unrealistic, arguing that solar and wind offer superior decarbonization economics at approximately $200 per ton of CO₂ compared to $350–400 for biofuels. He warned that without robust regulatory frameworks, the market for expensive biofuels cannot be sustained and cautioned against stranding biorefinery assets through diluted targets.

The announcement reflects broader tensions within the clean energy transition regarding policy credibility and investment economics. Airlines have pushed back against TotalEnergies, arguing the company is underinvesting in SAF capacity despite stating it could supply 10% SAF by 2030. This dispute underscores disagreements over the cost-effectiveness of mandated fuel transitions, with industry leaders weighing competing decarbonization strategies and the regulatory certainty needed to justify capital allocation to emerging clean fuel infrastructure.

title: IATA Chief Says EU Sustainable Aviation Fuel Targets Not Achievable
summary:
The International Air Transport Association's chief Willie Walsh stated Thursday that European Union targets requiring airlines to use 20 percent sustainable aviation fuel by 2035 are unachievable. Walsh cited insufficient SAF production and high costs as obstacles, noting that current usage stands at approximately two percent. The EU's regulatory framework mandates a gradual increase from two percent in 2025 to six percent by 2030 and ultimately 70 percent from 2050, while a European Commission spokesman maintained in March 2025 that these targets remain realistic and feasible.
 
 The airline industry faces production constraints and economic challenges in meeting decarbonization goals. Global SAF production is projected to reach only 2.4 million tonnes in 2025, representing 0.8 percent of total jet fuel consumption despite nearly doubling from 2024 levels. Despite his skepticism regarding SAF feasibility, Walsh reaffirmed that airlines remain committed to cutting emissions by half from 2005 levels by 2050.

title: DevvStream, Southern Energy, Frontline BioEnergy Expand Biomass-to-Jet Fuel Development
summary:
DevvStream Corp. announced a binding term sheet with Southern Energy Renewables and Frontline BioEnergy to advance biomass-to-jet fuel production and environmental-asset monetization. The collaboration involves expanding Frontline's demonstration facilities in Iowa with new pilot-scale units for bio-methanol and methanol-to-hydrocarbons production, with Southern Energy expected to invest up to $2.05 million to fund design, construction, and commissioning of these demonstration units.

The partnership aims to accelerate technical validation and commercialization of Southern's planned biomass-to-fuels platform in Louisiana. Biomass-to-jet fuel projects are gaining investor attention as airlines seek cleaner fuel options compatible with existing aircraft, supported by long-term supply agreements, government incentives, and emissions reduction mandates. Environmental credit generation from these facilities creates additional revenue streams, combining fuel sales with monetization of emissions reductions and renewable energy benefits.

title: Biomass-Based Sustainable Aviation Fuel Emerging as Viable Aviation Emissions Solution
summary:
Sustainable aviation fuel derived from biomass is gaining recognition as a practical approach to reducing aviation sector emissions, with airlines increasingly seeking cleaner fuel options compatible with existing aircraft. Long-term supply agreements between airlines and fuel producers, combined with government incentives and regulatory mandates for carbon reduction, are transforming these projects from experimental initiatives into established revenue-generating businesses with predictable cash flows.

The growth of this sector is attracting investor interest, with several companies positioned to capitalize on expanding demand. These developments reflect broader market recognition that biomass-to-jet fuel production offers a scalable pathway to decarbonizing aviation while meeting near-term sustainability requirements without requiring fleet-wide aircraft modifications.

title: UK Government Updates Low Carbon Hydrogen Standard and Approves First CCS Lease
summary: The Department for Energy Security and Net Zero published version four of the UK Low Carbon Hydrogen Standard, establishing revised methodology for calculating lifecycle greenhouse gas emissions from hydrogen production and updated compliance requirements for producers seeking government subsidies. The updates introduce 30-minute reporting intervals, expanded biomethane eligibility, simplified classification processes, new emissions calculators, and improved guidance across multiple technical areas to enhance clarity and streamline compliance procedures. The Crown Estate and Northern Endurance Partnership simultaneously signed the UK's first commercial-scale carbon capture and storage lease, securing rights to develop a saline aquifer carbon store approximately 140 kilometers offshore as part of the East Coast Cluster decarbonization infrastructure project, with operations expected to continue through the mid-2050s.

title: IMO Secretary General Urges Green Fuel Producers to Maintain Shipping Industry Focus
summary:
The International Maritime Organization's Secretary General Arsenio Dominguez appealed to producers of green fuels to continue pursuing the shipping sector as a market opportunity, despite the organization's October delay in voting on its Net Zero Framework. The postponement of the framework vote, which resulted from significant opposition at the UN body's Marine Environment Protection Committee, has raised concerns among some green fuel producers who are reconsidering their investment commitments and exploring alternative markets such as sustainable aviation fuel.

Dominguez acknowledged the obstacles facing the shipping industry's decarbonization pathway but emphasized the need for stakeholders to remain committed and address challenges through innovation and creativity. He declined to announce a new timeline for the Net Zero Framework vote, stating that his immediate priority is meeting with various country delegations before the MEPC's April meeting to assess their positions on the framework's implementation.

title: ACME Group Invests ₹12,422 Crore in Odisha Green Methanol Plant
summary:
ACME Group has signed an agreement with the Industrial Promotion and Investment Corporation of Odisha Limited to develop a green methanol production facility in Odisha, with an investment of ₹12,422 crore. The plant will produce approximately 200,000 tonnes of green methanol annually starting between 2029 and 2030, utilizing green hydrogen from renewable energy sources and captured carbon dioxide. The company is already engaged in discussions with global shipping companies for long-term supply contracts.

Green methanol is emerging as a critical low-carbon fuel alternative for international shipping, driven by stricter International Maritime Organization emission standards targeting net-zero emissions by 2050. This investment positions Odisha as a significant player in India's clean fuels sector and supports the country's National Green Hydrogen Mission. ACME Group's existing green hydrogen and ammonia projects in the state near Paradip and Gopalpur further strengthen the region's clean energy manufacturing capabilities.

title: Maharashtra Launches Three Billion Dollar Bamboo Biomethanol Production Initiative
summary:
The Government of Maharashtra signed a Memorandum of Understanding with Novel Biofuels and ACTUAL to develop a large-scale bamboo-to-biomethanol facility in eastern Maharashtra. The $3 billion project will establish a 1 million tonne per annum production capacity, converting approximately 3 million tonnes of sustainably managed bamboo annually into biomethanol for global shipping and aviation markets. The initiative integrates rural farmers and forestry communities into structured supply systems through long-term contracts and price-stability mechanisms.

The project positions Maharashtra and India as competitive suppliers of low-carbon fuels while anchoring agricultural communities directly into industrial value chains. By treating bamboo feedstock as strategic infrastructure and embedding farmers into energy production systems, the platform aims to support the state's broader trillion-dollar economic ambition while creating predictable income streams for rural populations and securing resilient fuel supplies for global markets.

title: Cycle0 Acquires Corte Pila Plant to Accelerate Italian Biomethane Production
summary:
Cycle0 has completed the acquisition of the Corte Pila biomethane facility in Italy, an operational plant that will transition from electricity generation to biomethane production by mid-2026. The plant will produce approximately 44 GWh annually of biomethane, which will be injected into a newly constructed gas grid injection point as part of Cycle0's €100 million investment program in the country.

The acquisition represents Cycle0's expansion across Europe, bringing its portfolio to nine plants planned or operational. Combined with a second facility under construction in Pontinia, the two Italian plants will collectively produce enough biomethane to supply approximately 6,000 households. The acquisition follows Cycle0's successful participation in a renewable gas auction and strengthens the company's position as a leading European biomethane operator while supporting local agricultural waste management and Italy's renewable energy transition.

title: Malaysia Introduces First Comprehensive National Climate Change Bill To Parliament
summary:
Malaysia's Deputy Minister of Natural Resources and Environmental Sustainability announced that the country's first comprehensive National Climate Change Bill will be presented to Parliament during the current legislative session, with its first reading expected imminently. The legislation has been in development for years and arrives alongside the finalization of Malaysia's National Carbon Market Policy, together creating a legal framework to regulate greenhouse gas emissions and establish carbon trading mechanisms aligned with the Paris Agreement.

The dual-track approach grants the government explicit authority to regulate carbon trading activities, including Internationally Transferred Mitigation Outcomes (ITMOs), which enable countries to cooperate on emissions reductions through bilateral and multilateral agreements. Malaysia has committed to reducing economy-wide carbon intensity by 45% by 2030 compared to 2005 levels, with the legislation supporting this target through enhanced measurement, reporting, and transparency mechanisms while implementing Article 6 provisions of the Paris Agreement.

title: Trump Administration Advances Sustainable Fuels Policy With New Investments And Standards
summary: President Trump signed an appropriations bill on January 23 containing provisions on biomass energy and sustainability, with Congressional guidance addressing sustainable aviation fuel and related energy standards. Clean Fuels Alliance America subsequently wrote to the president emphasizing the biodiesel, renewable diesel, and SAF sectors' importance to U.S. energy security and rural economies. A major investment commitment of up to $30 million was announced for a sustainable aviation fuel project beginning construction in 2026, while New Mexico became the fourth state to adopt a clean transportation fuel standard following its Environmental Improvement Board's unanimous approval on January 22. Additionally, shipping industry research from Denmark demonstrated climate benefits of biofuel use and was submitted to the International Maritime Organization.

title: Brazil's BNDES Backs Climate Funds Focused On Green Hydrogen And Biofuels
summary:
Brazil's National Development Bank (BNDES) has selected seven investment funds through its Climate Mitigation Call initiative, with a strategic focus on green hydrogen and biofuels development. The selection represents the bank's commitment to directing capital toward decarbonization technologies and sustainable fuel production pathways.

This initiative demonstrates BNDES's role in mobilizing private investment toward climate solutions, positioning Brazil as a key player in developing low-carbon hydrogen and advanced biofuel sectors. The fund selections align with broader efforts to transition energy systems away from fossil fuels while leveraging Brazil's existing expertise in renewable fuel production.

title: XCF Global DevvStream Southern Energy Announce Three-Party Merger Agreement
summary:
XCF Global, DevvStream Corp, and Southern Energy Renewables have agreed to a binding term sheet to combine into a single integrated platform focused on sustainable aviation fuel production and carbon credit monetization. The proposed merger aims to accelerate SAF development through HEFA and e-methanol pathways while expanding domestic production capacity and integrating environmental attribute strategies across North America and emerging markets. XCF has secured investor funding to complete upgrades at its New Rise Reno refinery, including mechanical, electrical, and process improvements needed to bring the facility into sustained commercial production.
The combined entity is intended to create a competitive low-carbon fuels platform that leverages XCF's production scale and speed-to-market capabilities, Southern's biomass feedstock expertise, and DevvStream's environmental-asset monetization strengths. The parties are also exploring opportunities to integrate small modular reactor nuclear power with electro-sustainable aviation fuel production and associated environmental attribute structures. Final completion of the transaction remains subject to definitive agreement negotiations and board approvals by all three companies.

title: Europe Cement Market Projected to Expand to $26 Billion by 2029
summary:
Research and Markets released a comprehensive report projecting the European cement industry will grow at a compound annual growth rate of 5.4% from 2025 to 2029, reaching approximately $26 billion by the end of the forecast period. The market is being driven by increased public infrastructure spending under the EU Green Deal, investments in climate-resilient building programs, and demand for decarbonization solutions. The report highlights that the region experienced a 6.1% annual growth rate during 2020-2024 and is expected to maintain positive momentum through 2029.

Europe's cement sector is undergoing significant transformation as companies prioritize carbon capture technologies, alternative fuels, and clinker substitution to comply with EU Emissions Trading System regulations and Carbon Border Adjustment Mechanism rules. While larger industry leaders are scaling decarbonization efforts and securing green financing, smaller and mid-tier producers face challenges related to compliance costs, permit processing, and export exposure. Strategic competitive advantage will accrue to firms that successfully integrate supply chain management digitalization with emissions reductions while navigating fragmented national regulatory frameworks.

title: Japan and Mongolia Host Forum on Article 6 Paris Agreement Implementation and JCM Project Development
summary:
Japan's Ministry of the Environment and Mongolia's Ministry of Environment and Climate Change will co-host a forum on February 27, 2026, in Ulaanbaatar focused on implementing Article 6 of the Paris Agreement through the Joint Crediting Mechanism (JCM). The hybrid event will bring together government officials, international organizations, and business representatives to discuss bilateral frameworks, project cycles, credit issuance procedures, and methodologies related to JCM development.

The forum will feature presentations on innovative technologies and nature-based solutions supporting Mongolia's decarbonization objectives, along with business pitch presentations and one-on-one matchmaking sessions between Japanese and Mongolian companies offering JCM-related technologies and solutions. The event aims to accelerate concrete project development and deepen stakeholder understanding of Article 6 implementation mechanisms within the international carbon market framework.

title: Carbon Quarterly Newsletter Covers Policy, Trading, and Litigation Developments
summary:
Carbon Quarterly, a newsletter focused on carbon policy, law, and innovation, released its thirteenth volume featuring analysis across multiple domains of carbon regulation. The issue includes legal examination of COP30 outcomes, coverage of Japan's movement toward mandatory emissions trading, updates on U.S. Department of Energy loan program changes, Singapore's greenwashing guidance, state-level carbon market updates, and regulatory developments in carbon trading infrastructure including CFTC actions on voluntary carbon credit derivatives and electricity binary options.

The newsletter addresses carbon policy developments at governmental and business levels, recognizing the expanding impact of greenhouse gas regulations on society. Contributions span legal analysis, policy assessment, and market developments, with particular attention to emerging regulatory frameworks, litigation matters affecting climate policy implementation, and financial instruments related to carbon and energy markets.

title: Biofuels Transition From Emerging Technology To Established Market Baseline
summary: Biofuels have shifted from theoretical discussion to practical implementation across corporate decarbonization strategies. Companies are now integrating biofuels as a standard component of fuel portfolios, treating them as proven solutions alongside carbon-intensity optimization, regulatory compliance planning, and risk management rather than experimental alternatives.

title: Topsoe and Tangshan Jinlihai Partner on Sustainable Aviation Fuel Production in China
summary:
Topsoe, a technology company, announced a collaboration with Tangshan Jinlihai to develop a sustainable aviation fuel (SAF) and renewable diesel production facility in Tangshan, China. The project is expected to avoid approximately 700,000 tonnes of CO2 equivalent annually when fully operational. The partnership represents Topsoe's sixth SAF production contract in China and demonstrates the company's expanding presence in the region's decarbonization efforts.

The facility will be constructed at the Caofeidian National Petrochemical Base with a planned investment of approximately US$210 million, with construction starting in the second half of 2026 and operations beginning in the second half of 2028. The project addresses rising global demand for SAF, as aviation seeks to meet net-zero targets by 2050, with industry projections indicating SAF production will reach 2.1 million tonnes in 2025 and needs to comprise over 10 percent of aviation fuel consumption by 2030.

title: XFuel Secures Series A Funding to Scale Low-Carbon Fuel Production
summary:
XFuel announced a Series A funding round led by venture capital firms Wagner Carbon, Audacy, Future Planet Capital, Light Ray Ventures, and Overlap Holdings, alongside strategic investments from maritime logistics leaders Nippon Yusen Kabushiki Kaisha (NYK Line) and Stolt-Nielsen's venture arm. The funding will accelerate commercial deployment of XFuel's Chemical Liquid Refining technology, which converts waste hydrocarbon liquids into clean drop-in transport fuels for shipping and other hard-to-decarbonize sectors.

XFuel and its new strategic investors plan to explore long-term fuel supply agreements, pilot programs, and joint ventures at major global ports. The collaboration will leverage MARPOL-regulated waste streams to produce cost-competitive, low-carbon fuels addressing immediate emissions reduction needs in maritime transport and industrial applications.

title: Fuel Ethanol Production Declines While Carbon Removal and Biofuel Markets Show Mixed Signals
summary: U.S. fuel ethanol production decreased 6% during the week ending January 16, according to Energy Information Administration data released January 22, while weekly ethanol stocks rose 5% and exports surged 83%. Separately, Gevo Inc. reported that its North Dakota facility has generated over 500,000 carbon dioxide removal certificates since launching carbon capture and storage operations in June 2022. Brazilian sugarcane ethanol sales increased in early December, though current harvest season production and processing levels remain below the prior year. Additionally, a planned merger between Union Pacific and Norfolk Southern faced regulatory obstacles when the Surface Transportation Board determined the application incomplete on January 16.

title: Bureau Veritas Acquires SPIN360 to Expand Fashion Supply Chain ESG Services
summary:
Bureau Veritas, a French testing and certification company, has agreed to acquire SPIN360, a Milan-based sustainability advisory firm that specializes in lifecycle analytics and ESG reporting for premium fashion and luxury brands. The acquisition strengthens Bureau Veritas' capabilities in consumer product sustainability as regulatory pressure on Scope 3 emissions disclosures and circularity requirements intensifies across Europe. SPIN360 brings proprietary tools for life cycle assessment, carbon footprinting, material benchmarking, and supply chain monitoring to complement Bureau Veritas' existing certification, laboratory testing, and auditing infrastructure.

The deal reflects growing demand among luxury brands for granular Scope 3 data and verified sustainability pathways that satisfy both investors and regulators. Fashion and luxury supply chains have become a priority segment for ESG due diligence because of their complex material sourcing, exposure to animal products, and rapidly evolving European disclosure rules including the corporate sustainability reporting directive. The acquisition will designate Milan as a global center of excellence for premium fashion and luxury within Bureau Veritas' consumer product portfolio, with SPIN360 bringing a customer base of approximately 30 specialists serving global brands and European industrial suppliers.

title: Neste Shares Surge Following Broker Upgrades On Strong Renewable Fuel Margins
summary:
Neste Oyj shares climbed 5.9% in Helsinki trading after Morgan Stanley and Goldman Sachs upgraded the company, citing stronger renewable fuel prices and improved margins. Morgan Stanley raised its price target to 25 euros from 18.80 euros, projecting around $650 per tonne for renewable product margins in fiscal 2026, while Goldman Sachs upgraded the stock to Buy, citing above-consensus estimates and favorable biofuels regulatory conditions in Europe. The analysts highlighted policy-driven demand and a rebound in renewable diesel margins as key drivers.

The stock movement comes ahead of Neste's financial results on February 5, with investors assessing whether the recent rally reflects new fundamentals or is already priced into the shares. Analysts are particularly focused on the gap between Neste's renewable fuel selling prices and feedstock costs, with expectations that strong European regulatory compliance demand and tight supply will sustain higher renewable product margins through 2026. However, one analyst downgraded the stock to Neutral following its recent appreciation.

title: Mexican State of Colima Launches Carbon Tax System Generating Millions in Revenue
summary:
Colima has implemented a comprehensive ecological tax on greenhouse gas emissions from fixed sources, projected to generate MX$51.9 million (US$2.94 million) in 2026. The Tax on the Emission of Greenhouse Gases and Compounds to the Atmosphere entered into force on January 1, 2025, representing the state's pioneering approach to carbon pricing that integrates voluntary certification programs, carbon offset mechanisms, and financial incentives for emissions reduction.

Colima becomes the ninth Mexican state to adopt carbon pricing mechanisms, reflecting a dramatic expansion of subnational climate policy across Mexico. Eleven Mexican states now enforce carbon taxes, with average pricing at MX$262 per metric ton of CO₂ equivalent, though rates vary significantly by jurisdiction. This development supports Mexico's national climate commitments to reduce greenhouse gas emissions by 35 percent unconditionally and up to 40 percent conditionally by 2030.

title: BP and Corteva Launch Etlas Joint Venture for Sustainable Biofuel Feedstocks
summary:
BP and Corteva Agriscience have established Etlas, a joint venture designed to produce sustainable crop-based feedstocks for renewable diesel and sustainable aviation fuel. The venture will cultivate canola, mustard, and sunflower on existing farmland during off-seasons for food crops, aiming to begin supply in 2027 and scale production to approximately one million tonnes annually by the mid-2030s, potentially generating over 800,000 tonnes of low-carbon fuels yearly.

The partnership combines BP's integrated downstream capabilities with Corteva's seed technology expertise to strengthen the biofuels value chain and support decarbonization in transportation sectors. Executive leadership has been assigned from both companies, signaling their commitment to long-term development of sustainable biofeedstocks without requiring expansion of agricultural land.

title: European Leaders Advocate Clean Energy, Venture Capital Firm Raises $249 Million
summary: UK Energy Secretary Ed Miliband and EU Energy Commissioner Dan Jørgensen emphasized clean energy's strategic importance for European autonomy and economic stability amid geopolitical market instability. The officials framed renewable energy not solely as an environmental imperative but as essential infrastructure to reduce dependence on imported fossil fuels and strengthen regional security. Separately, venture capital firm 2150 closed its second fund at $249 million to support sustainable city development startups, with its portfolio encompassing companies in carbon reduction, industrial decarbonization, energy, and circular economy sectors. The firm has already invested in seven new companies through Fund II and manages over $1 billion in assets across a global investor base spanning Europe, Asia, and North America. Additionally, biofuels producer EcoCeres reported its Malaysian facility now operates at 95% capacity, producing up to 420,000 metric tons of sustainable aviation fuel annually from used cooking oil, with initial shipments reaching European markets in December 2025.

title: New Mexico Launches Clean Transportation Fuel Program Market In Southwest
summary:
New Mexico's Environmental Improvement Board unanimously adopted final regulations for the Clean Transportation Fuel Program on January 22, establishing April 1, 2025 as the market opening date. The program introduces a market-based credit and deficit system requiring transportation fuel producers and importers to reduce the lifecycle carbon intensity of fuels by 20% by 2030 and 30% by 2040 relative to a 2018 baseline. Early credit generation and reporting begins immediately, with formal fuel pathway certification starting in July 2026 and initial compliance period through December 2026.

New Mexico becomes the fifth jurisdiction in North America to implement a clean fuel standard, joining California, Oregon, Washington, and Canada's federal and British Columbia programs. Transportation accounts for approximately 25% of statewide emissions, making it the second-largest emissions source. The program supports state climate targets of 45% emissions reduction by 2030 and net-zero by 2050 while promoting deployment of lower-carbon fuels including renewable diesel, biofuels, hydrogen, and electricity. Analysis projects renewable diesel demand reaching approximately 129 million gallons by 2030, potentially utilizing capacity from existing in-state refinery infrastructure.

title: JFLCO Acquires Majority Stakes in Refrigerant and Methane Gas Management Companies
summary:
J.F. Lehman & Company announced the acquisition of majority stakes in Reclamation Technologies USA and Tradewater LLC through its investment affiliate JFL Equity Investors VI, establishing a new environmental services platform. The combined company will specialize in the collection, processing, and destruction of refrigerant and methane gases, as well as the plugging of orphaned oil and gas wells, with operations headquartered in Chicago and Indianapolis.

The platform is designed to capitalize on growing market demand for specialized closed-loop environmental services driven by regulatory requirements and end-market needs. Tradewater generates carbon offset credits through its gas mitigation activities across compliance and voluntary carbon markets, while RTI provides refrigerant recovery and reclamation services. The combined entity will be led by Jeff Laborsky as CEO and Katelyn Imrie as President, with the platform positioned for organic growth and strategic acquisitions in the fragmented environmental services sector.

title: LanzaTech Wins Contract to Build Bio-Ethanol Plant in Uttar Pradesh
summary:
LanzaTech Global has been selected by Spray Engineering Devices Ltd to construct a carbon recycling facility in Uttar Pradesh that will convert sugarcane bagasse into ethanol and other fuels and chemicals. The project, part of SED's Smart Village initiative, will include a 24,000 tonnes per annum ethanol plant capable of processing up to 300 tonnes of bagasse daily and is expected to achieve approximately 19,200 tonnes of CO₂ equivalent annual abatement. The facility will represent the first in India to employ LanzaTech's technology for this specific application and is anticipated to begin operations within two years.

The plant will utilize syngas generated through oxygen-enriched air gasification of sugarcane bagasse, with LanzaTech's proprietary microbes converting carbon-rich gases into ethanol for use in sustainable aviation fuel and renewable diesel production. The project aligns with India's PM JI-VAN Yojana government programme supporting advanced bioethanol production and aims to reduce agricultural residue burning while generating nutrient-rich biochar for soil improvement. This development expands LanzaTech's operational presence in India, complementing its existing commercial-scale plant at Indian Oil Corporation's Panipat refinery.

title: Essar Green Mobility Plans Deployment Of 30,000 Clean Freight Trucks Across India
summary:
Essar Green Mobility announced plans to deploy 30,000 liquefied natural gas and electric trucks supported by 100 alternative fuel and charging hubs across India as part of a major clean freight initiative. The company secured $275 million in funding, including a $20 million investment from Zerodha co-founder Nikhil Kamath, to accelerate deployment of over 10,000 new trucks and expand refuelling and charging infrastructure. The integrated platform combines vehicle manufacturing, fleet operations, and fuel infrastructure to address barriers including high upfront costs and limited infrastructure that have hindered adoption of low-emission freight technologies.

The initiative is expected to reduce approximately one million tonnes of CO2 annually from freight operations, addressing a significant source of transport emissions in India's carbon-intensive logistics sector. Essar's dual-fuel strategy uses LNG-powered trucks for long-haul freight and electric vehicles for short-haul and urban logistics, with charging and battery-swapping facilities integrated into fuel hubs. The expansion aligns with government efforts to reduce emissions and promote cleaner fuels, though analysts note that sustained policy support and long-term private investment will be essential for large-scale adoption and achievement of India's climate targets.

title: UAE Establishes Sustainable Aviation Fuel Production Strategy Targeting European Exports
summary:
The United Arab Emirates has unveiled a national sustainable aviation fuel strategy targeting annual production of 700 million liters by 2030 across up to five facilities. The nation plans to export at least half of this output to mature policy markets such as the European Union, projecting cumulative revenues of $1.7 billion by 2030. Multiple production projects are underway, including MENA Biofuels' facility converting used cooking oil into SAF, a Masdar and Tadweer partnership converting municipal waste through gasification, and a $30 million investment by SAFFA in a Dubai-based producer expected to commence operations in late 2028.

The UAE's strategy encompasses diverse feedstock sources including recycled fats and oils, municipal solid waste, and halophytes, with particular emphasis on long-term power-to-liquid synthetic fuel production using green hydrogen and captured carbon dioxide. This approach aligns with EU regulations requiring synthetic SAF components in blended supplies from 2030, starting at 0.7% and escalating to 35% by 2035. Domestically, the UAE has set a voluntary target of 1% locally produced SAF to be blended into airport fuel supplies by 2031, while pursuing broader ambitions to become a major green hydrogen producer leveraging its solar energy capacity.

title: Oxford University Launches Climate Policy Monitor Tracking Global Emissions Regulations
summary: Helena has assumed the position of Executive Director at the Climate Policy Monitor, a new initiative launched by the University of Oxford in 2024. The organization evaluates alignment between policies and regulations across 37 jurisdictions covering over 85 percent of global emissions, leveraging analysis from a network of leading law firms. The Monitor's initial focus spans six domains: carbon credits, climate-related disclosure, green prudential rules, methane abatement, public procurement, and transition planning.

title: Indonesia Joins Coalition to Grow Carbon Markets as Eleventh Member
summary:
Indonesia formally joined the Coalition to Grow Carbon Markets, with Forestry Minister Raja Juli Antoni announcing the membership during a meeting at Standard Chartered's London headquarters on January 20, 2026. The announcement follows a high-level UK-Indonesia Strategic Partnership launch between President Prabowo Subianto and British Prime Minister Keir Starmer, positioning carbon markets as a critical mechanism for mobilizing climate finance and supporting economic growth in developing nations.

Indonesia's membership carries significant weight due to the country's vast natural capital, including the world's third-largest tropical rainforest covering 52.1 percent of its land area. The nation's ecosystems, particularly its peatland forests concentrated in Kalimantan, Sumatra, and Papua, store approximately 13 billion metric tons of carbon and can hold up to 20 times more carbon per hectare than other forest types. Through coalition participation, Indonesia commits to increasing demand for high-integrity carbon credits from forestry and nature-based solutions while supporting green growth.

title: Global Carbon Council and Saudi VCM Launch Strategic Partnership for Carbon Credits Trading
summary:
The Global Carbon Council and Saudi Arabia's Regional Voluntary Carbon Market Company announced a strategic partnership enabling GCC-certified carbon credits to be traded on VCM's exchange platform. The integration achieved strong market activity in its first month, with over 600,000 tonnes of GCC-certified credits traded, demonstrating regional demand for independently verified, high-integrity carbon credits and the platform's capacity for price discovery and transparent market access.

The Global Carbon Council is an international carbon crediting programme accredited by ICAO's CORSIA scheme and ICROA, positioning it as a gateway for climate finance in emerging markets. GCC-certified credits can support governments and companies in meeting climate objectives including Nationally Determined Contributions and Internationally Transferred Mitigation Outcomes. The availability of these credits on VCM's Saudi-based exchange, which launched in November 2024, expands access to globally recognized credits for regional and international buyers.

title: Verra Technical Manager Discusses Scaling Biochar Deployment Through Carbon Markets
summary:
Kranav Sharma, Technical Manager for Industrial Innovation at Verra, participated in a session at Delhi Climate Innovation Week focused on accelerating biochar deployment in India. The discussion examined strategies to leverage both voluntary and compliance carbon markets while maintaining environmental integrity, farmer participation, and economic feasibility. The session brought together project developers, standard-setting bodies, carbon finance specialists, government officials, corporate buyers, and agricultural organizations to transition biochar initiatives from pilot programs to scalable market-based models.

The session addressed practical implementation challenges including credible monitoring, reporting, and verification processes, integration with existing Indian agricultural systems, and strategies for generating buyer demand for carbon removal from biochar projects. The event emphasized the need to connect technical and market-based solutions to support both environmental outcomes and farmer economic interests through carbon market mechanisms.

title: EcoCeres Launches Sustainable Aviation Fuel Plant in Malaysia
summary:
EcoCeres Renewable Fuel opened a sustainable aviation fuel production facility in Tanjung Langsat, Malaysia, positioning itself to meet growing SAF demand across Asia. The plant will source feedstock including used cooking oils, animal fats, and palm oil from Malaysia, Southeast Asia, and China, with the company aiming to eventually source all materials from the Southeast Asian region. CEO Matti Lievonen highlighted the strategic location's advantages, including access to feedstock supplies, port infrastructure, and workforce capabilities.

While Europe currently dominates SAF consumption with mandates reaching 2% blending and targets of 6% by 2030, Asian nations including Singapore, South Korea, Malaysia, Indonesia, Hong Kong, China, and Japan are establishing or increasing their own SAF mandates. Japan aims for 10% blending by 2030, while Singapore and South Korea currently maintain 1% mandates. EcoCeres already supplies over 50% of its SAF output to European markets but is actively developing demand in South Korea, Singapore, Japan, Hong Kong, and China.

title: Honeywell and TruAlt Bioenergy Partner to Establish 80,000 TPA SAF Production Facility in India
summary:
Honeywell announced an agreement with TruAlt Bioenergy Limited to deploy Honeywell's Ethanol-to-Jet technology for producing 80,000 tons per annum of sustainable aviation fuel in India.
