The facility will represent one of India's first dedicated, grassroots SAF production operations using ethanol derived from renewable sources as feedstock. The technology can also be adapted to produce renewable diesel for road transport applications.

The collaboration supports India's government vision for self-reliance in domestic fuel production while addressing emissions reduction in the aviation sector. The project is expected to increase demand for bioethanol and generate higher incomes for farmers. Honeywell's modular renewable fuels technology approach enables faster deployment and installation compared to traditional methods, reducing project risk and accelerating operational timelines.

title: National Wealth Fund Commits Over £100 Billion to UK Economic Growth and Clean Energy Transition
summary:
The UK National Wealth Fund announced a strategic investment plan targeting more than £100 billion in deployment across companies, infrastructure, and supply chains. The Fund aims to create and support over 200,000 jobs while targeting 500 million tonnes of CO2 equivalent emissions reductions by 2050. Priority sectors include hydrogen, carbon capture and storage, nuclear, battery manufacturing, electric vehicles, offshore wind, and sustainable aviation fuels, with £5.8 billion committed to four critical sectors over the current strategy period.

The Fund's revised approach focuses on three core ambitions: accelerating investment in clean energy transition projects, expanding place-based investment across all UK nations, and strengthening strategic capabilities for national security. To date, the initiative has committed one-third of its capital, mobilized over £17 billion in private investment, and supported creation of more than 70,000 jobs. The strategy prioritizes addressing market gaps and attracting additional private financing across 25 identified growth sectors.

title: Topsoe Selected for Saudi Arabia's Yanbu Green Ammonia Project
summary:
Topsoe has been chosen as the ammonia technology licensor for ACWA Power's Yanbu Green Hydrogen Project in Saudi Arabia. The company will provide dynamic ammonia technology, engineering support, proprietary equipment, and catalysts to convert green hydrogen into green ammonia at industrial scale. The facility is targeted to reach final investment decision in 2026, with operations commencing in 2030 and expected to prevent approximately 1.7 million tons of annual CO2 equivalent emissions compared to conventional natural gas-based ammonia production.

The project represents a significant development in Saudi Arabia's renewable energy strategy and industrial transformation goals. Topsoe's dynamic ammonia technology enables production rates to adjust rapidly in response to renewable electricity fluctuations, reducing hydrogen storage requirements and lowering overall capital and operating costs. The initial phase includes mega-scale green ammonia units with combined capacity of 2,700 metric tons per day, positioning green ammonia as a key solution for decarbonizing energy-intensive industries and long-distance transport sectors.

title: Alto Ingredients Western Production Segment Drives Growth Through Renewable Fuels
summary:
Alto Ingredients' Western production segment generated $115 million in alcohol sales and $37 million in essential ingredients revenue during 2024, producing approximately 61 million gallons of alcohol and 514,600 tons of essential ingredients. Beginning in 2025, the segment will also produce liquid CO2. The segment benefits from premium ethanol pricing due to regional supply constraints, proximity to key markets, and exposure to California's Low Carbon Fuel Standard, which supports demand for low-carbon ethanol and provides more predictable earnings compared to Midwest-focused competitors.

Alto's Western facilities are strategically positioned to serve LCFS-compliant markets through shorter transportation distances and carbon-intensity reduction initiatives, reducing exposure to ethanol oversupply cycles and elevated transportation costs. This geographic and regulatory advantage translates into sustainable pricing power and margin expansion, supporting the company's shift toward higher-value, lower-carbon products. The stock has outperformed its industry by gaining 62.6% over the past year and currently trades at a price-to-earnings multiple of 16.38, below the industry average of 16.84.

title: ClearBlue Markets Issues Q4 2025 Global Carbon Markets Quarterly Report
summary:
ClearBlue Markets released its fourth-quarter 2025 State of the Global Carbon Markets report, providing comprehensive analysis of regulatory developments and market fundamentals across major carbon trading systems worldwide. The report examines policy evolution including California's Cap-and-Invest extension, the Canada-Alberta MOU, and the EU's 2040 Climate Target, while analyzing supply-demand drivers such as data center electricity impacts, electrification initiatives, and biofuel feedstock dynamics. The report also addresses capital allocation strategies and sustainability benchmarking to support decision-making in carbon markets.
 
 Key findings highlighted significant regional developments across North American and global markets. California Carbon Allowances remained stable as the Program Review stalled, while RGGI experienced volatility from elevated power demand related to data centers and artificial intelligence operations. The Voluntary Carbon Market demonstrated a structural "flight to quality" with high-integrity credits commanding a 46% price premium, while China's national carbon market recovered 30% by mid-November following regulatory stabilization measures. European Union Allowances gained momentum as investment funds increased positions, and progress was achieved on EU-UK ETS linkage discussions and the finalization of the EU's 2040 climate reduction target.

title: India's Budget 2026 Must Prioritize Energy Security Clean Technology Deployment
summary:
Dr. Mahesh Dalvi outlines an ESG-focused agenda for India's Union Budget 2026, calling for targeted fiscal and regulatory measures to strengthen energy security, accelerate clean technology adoption, enhance grid resilience, and support social sustainability. The column emphasizes that Budget 2026 arrives at a critical moment with the compliance carbon market launching in 2026 and India's 2030 climate targets approaching, requiring a shift from capacity expansion to system-level integration, domestic manufacturing, and supply chain resilience.
 
 The piece highlights India's progress toward its Panchamrit clean energy goals and identifies specific budget priorities including production-linked incentives for solar manufacturers, increased R&D allocation for green hydrogen, launch of a ₹38,900-crore carbon capture and storage programme, and support for nuclear energy deployment. The column frames Budget 2026 as instrumental in positioning India as an energy-secure, self-reliant economy while advancing its Net Zero transition and achieving its status as the world's third-largest economy.

title: Singapore Launches Voluntary Sustainable Aviation Fuel Demand Aggregation Trial
summary:
Singapore announced a voluntary initiative enabling corporations to collectively purchase sustainable aviation fuel, complementing an upcoming mandatory green jet fuel levy on all departing flights. The trial will be formalized through an agreement signed at the Changi Aviation Summit between the Civil Aviation Authority of Singapore, the Singapore Sustainable Aviation Fuel Company, and participating organizations. The country aims to achieve 1 percent sustainable jet fuel blending at its airports by 2026, escalating to 3-5 percent by 2030.
 
 The initiative addresses aviation's decarbonization through sustainable fuels derived primarily from waste materials such as used cooking oil, which can integrate with existing aircraft and infrastructure without modification. Sustainable aviation fuel is projected to deliver approximately 65 percent of the emissions reductions necessary for the aviation sector to reach net-zero targets by 2050, according to industry assessments. The summit will also feature nine additional agreements, including partnerships with the International Civil Aviation Organization for leadership development and training cooperation arrangements with Kenya, North Macedonia, and Thailand.

title: Miombo Restoration Alliance Launches Four Large-Scale Carbon Removal Projects
summary:
The Miombo Restoration Alliance, a public-private partnership established by Trafigura, NGO consortium partners, and 11 central and southern African governments, announced the launch of its first carbon removal projects across Mozambique, Zambia, Tanzania, and Malawi. The four projects represent over USD$1 billion in direct expenditure and investment over their lifetimes, with revenue-sharing agreements benefiting approximately 100,000 community members, participant farmers, and national governments.
 
 The projects are estimated to remove over 50 million tonnes of CO2 equivalent while restoring 675,000 hectares of Miombo woodland through native species restoration and agroforestry activities. Credits are generated under Article 6 of the Paris Agreement framework. The Alliance, launched at New York Climate Week 2024, has strengthened its partner network in 2025 by adding Carbon Direct as scientific and project design advisor and Terraspect as payments infrastructure provider to support traceable local disbursements.

title: Concrete Chemicals Secures €350 Million Funding for Germany's Largest e-SAF Production Plant
summary:
Concrete Chemicals GmbH has received €350 million in public funding approved at national and regional levels to construct Germany's largest industrial-scale facility for producing Electro-Sustainable Aviation Fuel (e-SAF) in Schwedt, Brandenburg. The funding, operating under the EU's Climate Energy and Environmental Aid Guidelines framework, supports the development of clean hydrogen infrastructure and renewable fuel production capacity aligned with European climate and energy sovereignty objectives.
 
 The facility will employ a Power-to-Liquid process utilizing biogenic carbon dioxide and renewable hydrogen to produce over 37,000 tonnes annually of synthetic aviation fuel and e-naphtha, with projected annual fossil CO2 reductions exceeding 100,000 tonnes. The project directly supports the ReFuelEU Aviation Regulation's blending mandates, contributes to Germany's hydrogen infrastructure strategy, and enables future integration into the European Hydrogen Core Network, positioning the facility as a cornerstone of decarbonization efforts in European aviation and industrial fuel supply chains.

title: Lao PDR Adopts Carbon Credit Decree to Enable Private Sector Investment
summary:
The Government of Lao PDR signed and disseminated the Carbon Credit Decree on January 29, 2026, establishing a regulatory framework for carbon market activities and private sector participation. Prime Minister Sonexay Siphandone approved the decree, which was presented at a National Dissemination Workshop in Vientiane attended by government officials, private sector representatives, and development partners. The framework requires carbon project developers to obtain approvals from relevant line ministries before registering with the Ministry of Agriculture and Environment, while also regulating carbon-related businesses including project development, consulting, and market facilitation services.
 
 The decree positions Lao PDR as an emerging destination for climate-smart investment by providing legal certainty and transparent oversight mechanisms for carbon credit generation. Government officials emphasized that the policy aims to mobilize funds, create employment, and deliver benefits to local communities while ensuring alignment with international best practices. The framework leverages Lao PDR's forest resources, renewable energy potential, and land-use sectors for nature-based solutions and low-carbon investments in response to growing global demand for high-quality carbon credits.

title: Assam Petro-Chemicals APL Establishes E-Methanol Production Facility at Kandla Port
summary:
Assam Petro-Chemicals Ltd signed a memorandum of understanding with Deendayal Port Authority to develop a 150 tonnes per day e-methanol production plant at Kandla Port in Gujarat. The project represents a ₹1,200 crore capital investment and is expected to generate over 3,500 direct and indirect employment opportunities. The facility will produce electro-methanol using green hydrogen and captured carbon dioxide powered by renewable electricity, creating an integrated value chain for green marine fuels at the port.
 
 The initiative aligns with India's maritime decarbonization strategy and positions Kandla Port as a major green fuel supply hub for international shipping routes, including the Singapore-Rotterdam corridor. Port-based production of e-methanol offers operational advantages including reduced logistics costs and seamless integration with shipping demand. The facility will enable vessels to meet international emissions regulations while supporting India's transition toward cleaner shipping and sustainable economic growth.

title: Asia-Pacific Carbon Markets Entering New Phase Of Institutionalization And Implementation
summary:
A webinar hosted by RECESSARY on January 28 examined the evolving landscape of carbon markets across Asia-Pacific, with speakers discussing how pricing mechanisms, credit quality standards, and cross-border alignment are becoming central considerations for regional operations. Key developments highlighted include Singapore's planned 80% carbon tax increase to SGD 45 per tonne in 2026, Thailand's integration of its carbon tax and emissions trading scheme under a unified legal framework, and Vietnam's launch of a pilot ETS in late 2025.
 
 The discussion underscored a significant shift in market dynamics where governments continue refining policy designs while buyers increasingly prioritize carbon credit quality over volume. Notable milestones include the International Civil Aviation Organization's approval of premium Thai carbon credits for use under CORSIA and the completion of the first Article 6.2 internationally transferred mitigation outcome transaction between Thailand and Japan, both signaling movement toward cross-border credit integration and linking domestic markets to international frameworks.

title: China Establishes World's Largest Carbon Emissions Trading Market During Five-Year Plan
summary:
China's Ministry of Ecology and Environment announced significant environmental achievements during the 14th Five-Year Plan period from 2021 to 2025. The country established the world's largest carbon emissions trading market, which accumulated 865 million tonnes in trading volume and generated 57.663 billion yuan in transaction value by the end of 2025. Additionally, China built the largest clean power supply system and largest clean steel production system globally, achieved over 50 percent market penetration for new energy vehicles, and reached 78 percent clean transportation share in key regions and industries.
 
 The ministry highlighted broader climate action initiatives undertaken during this period, including the launch of a national voluntary greenhouse gas emissions trading market and the establishment of a product carbon footprint management system. China also issued its National Adaptation Strategy to Climate Change 2035 to strengthen climate resilience across key sectors. These developments reflect the country's commitment to accelerating green and low-carbon economic transition through policy frameworks and market mechanisms.

title: Nigeria Establishes Carbon Market Framework to Finance Energy Transition
summary:
Nigeria has implemented a national carbon registry and emissions reporting system designed to monetize verified emissions reductions and channel revenues into energy infrastructure, renewable deployment, methane abatement, and grid modernization. The framework anchors carbon market activities within Nigeria's Energy Transition Plan targeting net-zero emissions by 2060, with natural gas expected to comprise over 70% of the near-term energy mix while complementary initiatives like the Lagos Carbon Exchange aim to issue millions of credits and reinvest proceeds into clean energy and climate-resilient infrastructure.
 
 The Nigerian model presents a development finance mechanism for emerging economies facing capital constraints in energy transitions. Caribbean nations experiencing similar challenges—high imported fuel dependence, elevated electricity costs, and climate vulnerability—are examining comparable carbon market approaches. Countries including Guyana, Suriname, and Belize have already advanced forest carbon credits and blue-economy financing, positioning carbon markets as revenue-generating solutions that reduce reliance on traditional climate finance while supporting regional energy modernization goals.

title: European Renewable Fuel Targets Drive Hydrotreated Vegetable Oil Demand Growth
summary:
Stricter European renewable fuel mandates are expected to significantly boost demand for hydrotreated vegetable oil (HVO) throughout 2026, despite recent price declines from October highs. Germany plans to nearly double HVO demand to 2.6 billion liters this year by eliminating double-counting provisions for advanced biofuels, while the Netherlands is shifting to a greenhouse gas savings mandate. Overall European HVO demand is projected to reach 9.4 billion liters in 2026, up from 6.9 billion liters in 2025, as suppliers increasingly turn to HVO to comply with the EU Fuel Quality Directive's blending limits on conventional biodiesel.
 
 Market activity reflects growing interest in HVO as a drop-in diesel substitute that can be blended in larger volumes than traditional methyl ester biodiesel. Spot trading volumes surged to 144,000 tons in 2025 compared with 44,000 tons in 2024, while futures trading on the Intercontinental Exchange reached 5 million contracts. Regulatory changes are attracting continued supply flows from North America and China, with January 2026 already showing strong trade initiation volumes as market participants position for the anticipated demand surge from tightening European compliance requirements.

title: Spain Launches Public Consultation for First Floating Offshore Wind Auction
summary: Spain's Ministry for the Ecological Transition opened a public consultation period extending to February 24 to solicit feedback on the design of the nation's inaugural competitive auction for floating offshore wind capacity. The consultation will inform the final structure of the auction mechanism, with submissions invited from companies, industry associations, and public authorities. The auction framework will employ a two-sided Contract for Difference model and incorporate non-economic evaluation criteria alongside price considerations, including environmental impact, job creation, and local industrial development. The Spanish government has also implemented industrial support measures to prepare ports and logistics infrastructure for offshore wind deployment.

title: UK Government Launches Consultation on Carbon Capture Non-Pipeline Transport Policy
summary: The UK Department for Energy Security and Net Zero issued a consultation on February 5, 2026, seeking public input on policy frameworks for non-pipeline transport (NPT) of captured carbon dioxide. The consultation, open until May 1, 2026, addresses delivery mechanisms, cost support structures, regulatory environments, and operational standards for NPT solutions. The government is soliciting responses from project developers, infrastructure providers, supply chain companies, investors, and academics with interest in CCUS deployment across the UK and offshore waters.

title: PG&E Deploys At-The-Meter Technology To Reduce EV Adoption Costs
summary:
Pacific Gas and Electric Company announced a partnership with SPAN to deploy SPAN Edge devices through its new PanelBoost program, enabling residential customers to adopt electric vehicles and appliances without expensive electrical panel upgrades. The at-the-meter solution uses dynamic load management technology to allow homes to add high-efficiency electric equipment while maintaining grid reliability. PG&E estimates over 600,000 homes in its service area will require some form of electric service upgrade within the next decade to meet electrification demand.

The PanelBoost program addresses a significant barrier to residential electrification by reducing infrastructure upgrade costs and timelines for customers. SPAN Edge uses Dynamic Service Rating capability to shape home energy demand during peak events and protect local transformers. Early feedback from electricians and customer testers indicated strong support for the solution as an alternative to traditional panel upgrades. The deployment builds on lessons learned from PG&E's SAVE virtual power plant program and represents part of the utility's broader grid-edge innovation strategy.

title: France Renewable Energy Association Urges Government Action on Offshore Wind
summary:
France renouvelables, the country's renewable electricity industry association, has called on the French government to accelerate its offshore wind development framework to align with broader European ambitions. The association highlighted delays in the A09 and A010 auction frameworks and the postponed publication of France's third Multiannual Energy Programming Plan (PPE3), which is intended to guide energy strategy through 2035 and beyond. At the January 2026 North Seas Summit in Hamburg, European countries collectively reaffirmed targets to develop 300 gigawatts of offshore wind capacity by 2050, a volume sufficient to meet approximately one-third of EU electricity demand, reduce fossil fuel imports by €70 billion annually, and cut carbon emissions by 15 percent yearly.

France renouvelables warned that delays in publishing the PPE3 and auction specifications risk undermining France's position as an energy power and threaten future investment in the offshore sector. The association emphasized that rapid government action is essential for France to contribute meaningfully to European energy security and decarbonization objectives. This call aligns with earlier statements from France Offshore Renewables, a regional clean energy cluster body, which flagged mounting delays and inconsistent decision-making that could jeopardize offshore wind projects and investor confidence.

title: IRS Issues Safe Harbor for Carbon Sequestration Tax Credits Amid EPA Reporting Changes
summary:
The Internal Revenue Service released Notice 2026-1 on December 19, 2025, establishing a temporary safe harbor for taxpayers claiming the federal income tax credit for qualified carbon oxide sequestration under section 45Q. The notice addresses a compliance gap created by the EPA's proposed repeal of greenhouse gas reporting requirements that are currently necessary to substantiate the credit. The safe harbor applies to taxpayers disposing of qualified carbon oxides through Class VI injection wells or ceased oil reservoirs during 2025, and is triggered only if the EPA does not launch its electronic Greenhouse Gas Reporting Tool by June 10, 2026.

Under the safe harbor, taxpayers may satisfy monitoring, reporting, and verification obligations through independent certification by a state-registered engineer or geologist rather than EPA's subpart RR reporting mechanism. Taxpayers must maintain compliance activities, compile annual reports with required documentation, engage qualified independent certification, and file Form 8933 with their tax returns by normal filing deadlines. Treasury and the IRS indicated this is interim guidance pending issuance of proposed regulations addressing the regulatory uncertainty created by the EPA's proposed rule changes.

title: DOE Leases Dormant Nuclear Facility to General Matter for HALEU Enrichment
summary:
The Department of Energy has entered into a lease agreement with General Matter to evaluate and potentially restore the Fuel Manufacturing and Enrichment Facility (FMEF) at the Hanford site in Washington state. The 190,000-square-foot facility, completed in 1984 but never operationalized, has been in dormant status since 1993. General Matter will conduct site characterization, assess facility upgrades, and engage with local stakeholders as part of the evaluation process to return the facility to service.

This lease represents part of a broader DOE initiative to develop domestic uranium enrichment capacity, particularly for High-Assay Low-Enriched Uranium (HALEU). General Matter received a $900 million task order from the DOE in the preceding month to accelerate HALEU enrichment capacity development. The company is simultaneously developing a new enrichment facility at the former Paducah Gaseous Diffusion Plant in Kentucky, with construction expected to begin in 2026 and operations targeted for 2034.

title: Green Hydrogen Project in South Taranaki Begins Construction After Five Year Delay
summary:
Construction has commenced on a green hydrogen facility in South Taranaki, New Zealand, following nearly five years of regulatory delays since $19.9 million in Provincial Growth Fund financing was announced in 2020. The project integrates four 206-metre wind turbines to supply renewable electricity to Ballance Agri-Nutrients' Kapuni facility and produce green hydrogen for Hiringa Energy's zero-emission transport refuelling operations, with total project costs reaching $112.3 million through co-funding from partners. The development is expected to create approximately 50 construction jobs and seven permanent positions.

Regional Development Minister Shane Jones highlighted the project's significance as one of New Zealand's first large-scale integrated wind and green hydrogen production facilities, positioning it as a key economic diversification initiative for Taranaki. The project faced earlier opposition from Te Korowai o Ngāruahine Trust and Greenpeace on Treaty, cultural, and environmental grounds, but both the High Court and Court of Appeal ruled against the appeal, clearing the path for construction to proceed.

title: Michigan Files Antitrust Lawsuit Against Major Oil and Gas Companies for Suppressing Renewables
summary:
Michigan's Attorney General filed a federal antitrust lawsuit against BP, Chevron, ExxonMobil, Shell, and the American Petroleum Institute, alleging a decades-long conspiracy to suppress renewable energy technology and electric vehicle adoption. The complaint claims these companies coordinated efforts to delay the energy transition, reduce competition, raise consumer prices, and protect fossil fuel market dominance through patent litigation, disinformation campaigns, and diversion of capital away from clean technologies.

The lawsuit invokes the Sherman Antitrust Act, Clayton Antitrust Act, and Michigan Antitrust Reform Act. Michigan's Attorney General cited an energy affordability crisis and argued that elevated costs stem from corporate greed rather than natural economic factors. The filing references internal documents, including claims that ExxonMobil recognized climate risks and renewable energy requirements in 1979 but chose to strategically obstruct the energy transition instead.

title: Bulgaria Secures EU Funding For 250-Kilometer Hydrogen Pipeline to Greece
summary:
Bulgaria's state-owned gas transmission operator Bulgartransgaz received $5.39 million in European Union financing to construct a 250-kilometer hydrogen transmission route connecting Sofia to the Greek border at Kulata. The project represents the first phase of Bulgaria's hydrogen backbone infrastructure and integrates into the broader South-East European Hydrogen Corridor, a multinational initiative designed to link hydrogen networks across Greece, Bulgaria, Romania, Hungary, Slovakia, Czechia, and Germany.

The hydrogen corridor initiative positions hydrogen and natural gas as central components of the region's future energy system. According to Bulgartransgaz leadership, the infrastructure development is intended to enable reliable and cost-effective energy supply across southeastern Europe. The project advances cross-border energy infrastructure coordination and regional hydrogen transport capability.

title: Major Carbon Polluters Shift Spending Toward Verified Green Projects
summary:
Corporate emitters are increasingly directing voluntary carbon market investments toward projects with independently verified climate benefits, according to two recent reports. This marks a significant shift away from funding initiatives with questionable environmental impact, driven by mounting criticism over greenwashing and integrity concerns in the voluntary carbon offset sector. Rating agencies and verification programs now help corporations assess project quality using standardized metrics similar to bond ratings.

The movement reflects growing pressure on corporations to demonstrate genuine climate impact rather than symbolic action. Companies like BeZero Carbon have emerged to provide third-party verification of emissions reductions, creating standards that guide corporate funding decisions. This trend indicates the voluntary carbon market is developing more rigorous evaluation mechanisms in response to widespread skepticism about project effectiveness and corporate climate claims.

title: Lunar Energy Raises $230 Million to Expand Home Battery and Virtual Power Plant Operations
summary:
Lunar Energy announced it secured $230 million in combined Series C and Series D funding to accelerate manufacturing capacity and software deployment. The funding round, led by B Capital, Prelude Ventures, and Activate Capital, brings the company's total capital raised since 2020 to approximately $530 million. The company plans to quadruple its home battery manufacturing capacity and expand operations from California into Illinois, Nevada, Texas, and Utah, while scaling its virtual power plant software business to utilities and energy retailers.

The company currently manufactures battery systems for approximately 10,000 homes annually and manages 650 megawatts of distributed devices. Lunar Energy's growth reflects broader market momentum in distributed battery storage and virtual power plant aggregation, positioning home batteries as grid assets to reduce reliance on costly new generation and transmission infrastructure. The residential battery market remains early-stage, with fewer than 1 million of the 75 million U.S. homes currently equipped with storage systems.

title: Potomac Edison Launches Electric School Bus Pilot Program With V2G Testing
summary:
Potomac Edison received Maryland Public Service Commission approval to launch an $11.1 million pilot program supporting local school systems in transitioning to electric buses. The program will provide incentives for up to 28 electric school buses, cover the cost difference between diesel and electric vehicles (approximately $250,000 per bus), fund charging infrastructure and electrical upgrades, and offer technical and administrative support to participating districts. The initiative runs for five years or until funds are exhausted, beginning in early 2026.

The program directly supports Maryland's Climate Solutions Now Act of 2022, which requires public school systems to procure only zero-emission vehicles. Beyond financial assistance, Potomac Edison will pilot vehicle-to-grid (V2G) technology to test how energy stored in bus batteries can be discharged back to the electrical grid during non-operational periods, potentially enhancing grid reliability and supporting emergency response across the utility's service territory in seven Maryland counties.

title: Article 6 Observatory Launches to Support Paris Agreement Carbon Market Implementation
summary:
The Article 6 Observatory, an independent platform, launched today to provide transparency and accountability support for the operationalization of Article 6 of the Paris Agreement. The observatory will track cooperative approaches under Article 6.2, monitor the Article 6.4 Paris Agreement Crediting Mechanism as it approaches implementation, and maintain a resource hub of independent research and peer-reviewed materials on Article 6-related topics.

The platform addresses the need for stakeholder guidance as international cooperative approaches advance and the Article 6.4 crediting mechanism prepares for deployment. The NewClimate Institute is participating as part of the observatory's steering committee, contributing expertise to support the platform's development and evolution.

title: Kochi Airport To Operate India's First Hydrogen-Powered Passenger Buses
summary:
Cochin International Airport Ltd signed a Memorandum of Agreement with the Kerala Hydrogen Valley Innovation Cluster Foundation on February 1 to procure and operate three hydrogen fuel cell electric buses at Kochi airport. The Kerala HVIC Foundation will provide financial support capped at ₹2.90 crore per bus, with a total project cost of ₹8.7 crore to be released in stages. Bus procurement is expected within 12 months, after which CIAL will assume ownership and operational responsibility.

The initiative is part of India's National Green Hydrogen Mission and positions Kochi airport as a leader in clean energy adoption within the aviation sector. Hydrogen fuel supply will come from a green hydrogen plant jointly developed by CIAL and BPCL that is scheduled to commence operations soon. Statutory approvals for hydrogen supply infrastructure are already in place, enabling rapid project implementation and reducing emissions from airport ground transportation.

title: France and China Launch First Green Shipping Corridor Between Le Havre and Ningbo
summary:
Haropa Port and Ningbo Zhoushan Port have signed a strategic agreement with MSC, TiL, Bureau Veritas, and Chinese maritime authorities to establish a green shipping corridor connecting Le Havre and Ningbo. The corridor will include shore power infrastructure, terminal electrification, low-carbon hinterland connections, and alternative fuel supply chains featuring bio-LNG, green ammonia, and hydrogen, with onboard carbon capture pilots also under consideration. Bureau Veritas will provide technical certification and support.

The route addresses a significant trade relationship, with China representing 30 percent of Haropa's container traffic and Ningbo Zhoushan Port handling 22 percent of that volume. The partnership represents a practical implementation mechanism for the International Maritime Organization's 2050 decarbonization targets and expands existing Franco-Chinese maritime cooperation efforts.

title: Google Signs 100 MW Offshore Wind Agreement With EnBW In Germany
summary:
Google has secured a 15-year power purchase agreement with German utility EnBW for 100 megawatts of capacity from the He Dreiht offshore wind farm in the North Sea. The 960 MW project, located approximately 85 kilometres northwest of Borkum, began turbine installation in 2024, achieved first power in November 2025, and expects full commissioning by summer 2026. EnBW has already contracted most of the project's output to multiple corporate buyers including DHL Group, Evonik, and Frankfurt Airport operators.

The agreement reflects the growing role of long-term corporate power purchase agreements in financing large-scale renewable infrastructure while meeting corporate carbon-free energy commitments. For Germany, such contracts provide revenue certainty to developers undertaking capital-intensive offshore construction while supporting national decarbonization objectives tied to industrial electrification and digital infrastructure expansion. The deal underscores how technology companies' rising electricity demand, particularly from artificial intelligence and data centres, is driving direct renewable energy procurement.

title: Gevo Verity and Bushel Integrate Farm Data Platform for Carbon Intensity Scoring
summary:
Verity Holdings, a subsidiary of Gevo, and Bushel announced an integration connecting Bushel's on-farm data collection with Verity's sustainability modeling platform to document agricultural practices and calculate carbon intensity scores. The system allows farmers to voluntarily share verified production data through secure, permission-based channels to support eligibility and credit value under Section 45Z clean fuel tax credits, with the Gevo North Dakota ethanol facility serving as the first pilot customer.

The integration addresses industry demand for transparent, traceable grain sourcing and positions agricultural suppliers to participate in emerging carbon markets and Section 45Z compliance programs. The partnership reflects collaboration under a North Dakota Clean Sustainable Energy Authority grant and responds to Treasury Department guidance on verifiable carbon intensity data requirements for low-carbon fuel production credits.

title: Indian Automakers Press Government To Finalize Stringent Vehicle Emission Norms
summary:
Indian automobile manufacturers are urging the government to quickly finalize Corporate Average Fuel Efficiency (CAFÉ) norms scheduled to take effect in April 2027, citing the need for regulatory certainty to guide investment and product development decisions. The Society of Indian Automobile Manufacturers submitted a formal representation in November 2025 requesting clarity on the proposed standards, and Union Minister Nitin Gadkari assured industry stakeholders in late January that the final rules would be notified by month-end, though the notification remains pending as of early February.

Automakers require multiple years to redesign vehicle platforms, introduce electrified powertrains, and implement efficiency improvements needed to meet tightened fuel economy requirements. The regulatory uncertainty creates planning challenges across powertrain development, supply chain sourcing, and product launch schedules, with manufacturers concerned about balancing potential over-investment against the risk of insufficient preparation. Industry executives emphasize the urgency of receiving final targets with approximately 13 months remaining before the compliance deadline.

title: Canada Cancels EV Mandate, Reintroduces $5,000 Purchase Incentive
summary:
Prime Minister Mark Carney announced a revised automotive strategy that cancels the federal EV mandate requiring 100 percent electric vehicle sales by 2035, replacing it with a flexible emissions-reduction framework targeting 75 percent EV adoption by 2035 and 90 percent by 2040. The government will reintroduce a $5,000 cash incentive for EV purchases beginning February 16, declining to $2,000 by 2030, alongside $2.3 billion in consumer incentives, $1 billion for charging infrastructure, and $3 billion in industry tax credits.

The policy shift responds to automaker concerns that the original mandate was overly rigid and costly, while addressing competitive pressures from U.S. tariffs and challenges in the EV transition. The government aims to support the purchase of 840,000 vehicles over five years while positioning Canada's auto sector through targeted manufacturing incentives and infrastructure investment.

title: U.K. Funds Green Aviation Projects as U.S. Advances Clean Fuel Policy
summary: The U.K. Department of Transport announced £43 million in funding for green aviation projects focused on clean fuels and zero-emission aircraft technology. Concurrently, the White House Office of Management and Budget completed its review of proposed rules implementing the 45Z clean fuel production credit, and President Trump signed an appropriations bill containing provisions related to biomass energy, sustainability, and sustainable aviation fuel guidance. Industry groups including the Iowa Corn and Iowa Renewable Fuels Association called for expanded E15 fuel access, while representatives from the U.S. Green Building Council and renewable fuel organizations met with Canadian energy and transportation officials to advance clean fuel initiatives across North America.

title: Republican Lawmakers Plan Task Force for Year-Round E15 Ethanol Fuel Sales
summary:
President Trump called on Congress to finalize a deal permitting year-round sales of E15 ethanol-blended gasoline in the United States, stating that House Speaker Mike Johnson and Senate Leader John Thune are working toward an agreement that would benefit corn farmers, consumers, and refiners. Trump made the comments during a speech in Iowa, reiterating his campaign promise to support expanded E15 availability. The push follows a failed attempt by Midwestern Republicans to include year-round E15 provisions in a government funding package, prompting GOP leaders to establish a rural energy working group to negotiate between ethanol and oil industry interests.

Simultaneously, the White House Office of Management and Budget completed its review of a proposed rule implementing guidance for the 45Z clean fuel production tax credit on January 23. The credit, established by the Inflation Reduction Act of 2022 and extended under the One Big Beautiful Bill Act, provides tax incentives for production and sale of low-emission transportation fuels. The OMB held 16 stakeholder meetings in January and modified the proposed rule during review, with a notice of proposed rulemaking scheduled for public comment release in May.

title: Trump Calls Congress to Finalize Year-Round E15 Fuel Agreement for Farmers
summary:
President Trump urged Congress to resolve disagreements over year-round E15 fuel sales during a speech in Iowa, expressing confidence that House Speaker Mike Johnson and Senate Leader John Thune are close to reaching a deal that would benefit corn farmers, consumers, and refiners. The president's explicit backing positions the White House firmly with ethanol and biofuels advocates in ongoing negotiations between Republican lawmakers, agricultural groups, and the oil and refining industry.

Simultaneously, the White House Office of Management and Budget completed its review of a proposed rule implementing guidance for the 45Z clean fuel production tax credit. The rule, originally submitted by the Treasury Department and IRS in December, underwent modifications during the review process and is expected to move toward public comment in May, with the OMB having conducted 16 stakeholder meetings in January to discuss the proposed guidance.

title: Air Products Awarded Over $140 Million NASA Liquid Hydrogen Supply Contract
summary:
Air Products secured supply contracts from NASA valued at more than $140 million to provide liquid hydrogen to multiple NASA facilities. The company will deliver approximately 36.5 million pounds of liquid hydrogen to Kennedy Space Center and Cape Canaveral Space Force Station in Florida, as well as Marshall Space Flight Center in Alabama and Stennis Space Center in Mississippi.

The contract represents a significant procurement agreement for aerospace-grade hydrogen supply infrastructure. Air Products will service NASA's largest hydrogen sphere at Kennedy Space Center and support the agency's operational requirements across multiple research and launch facilities.

title: Air Products Awarded NASA Contracts Totaling Over 140 Million Dollars
summary:
Air Products announced it has secured supply contracts from NASA exceeding $140 million to deliver liquid hydrogen to multiple NASA facilities, including the Kennedy Space Center, Cape Canaveral Space Force Station, Marshall Space Flight Center, and Stennis Space Center. The contract involves supplying approximately 36.5 million pounds of liquid hydrogen to support space launch operations and engine testing programs across these locations.

The agreement represents a continuation of Air Products' decades-long partnership with NASA dating back to 1957, during which the company has supplied industrial gases for the Apollo program, Space Shuttle missions, and upcoming Artemis II operations. Air Products recently completed filling the world's largest hydrogen sphere at Kennedy Space Center, which measures 90 feet in height and 83 feet in diameter, requiring over 730,000 gallons of liquid hydrogen delivered via more than 50 trailer loads.

title: Air Products Secures Over $140 Million Liquid Hydrogen Supply Contract with NASA
summary: Air Products and Chemicals announced a multi-year supply agreement valued at over $140 million to deliver approximately 36.5 million pounds of liquid hydrogen to NASA facilities across the United States. The contract covers supply to Kennedy Space Center and Cape Canaveral Space Force Station in Florida, Marshall Space Flight Center in Alabama, and Stennis Space Center in Mississippi. Air Products' leadership emphasized the company's decades-long track record of reliably supplying large-scale quantities of liquid hydrogen and other industrial gases through established supply chain networks.

title: Sankla Renewables Signs Agreement For Rs15,000 Crore Sustainable Aviation Fuel Project
summary:
Sankla Renewables, a clean-energy subsidiary of Sankla Buildcoon, has entered into a memorandum of understanding with the Government of Maharashtra to develop a sustainable aviation fuel manufacturing facility at Chalisgaon in Jalgaon district. The agreement was signed during the World Economic Forum in Davos and represents the company's expansion from real estate into clean energy manufacturing with a planned investment of Rs15,000 crore.

The proposed facility will convert agricultural waste including sugarcane bagasse, cotton stalks, soybean husks, and pigeon pea residue into aviation fuel using internationally approved technologies. The project is expected to achieve full operational capacity by 2029 through a phased development approach, create over 3,000 direct and indirect jobs, and operate entirely on renewable energy with comprehensive water recycling and zero liquid waste discharge. The initiative aligns with national objectives for reducing fuel imports, enhancing energy security, and lowering aviation sector emissions while generating supplementary income for rural farming communities through agricultural waste procurement.

title: Sankla Buildcoon Signs MoU To Develop Rs 15000 Crore Sustainable Aviation Fuel Plant
summary:
Sankla Buildcoon's clean-energy subsidiary Sankla Renewables has entered into a Memorandum of Understanding with the Maharashtra Government to establish a Rs. 15,000 crore Sustainable Aviation Fuel manufacturing facility in Chalisgaon, Jalgaon District. The agreement was formalized at the World Economic Forum in Davos and represents the company's strategic expansion from real estate development into large-scale clean energy infrastructure. The proposed facility will convert agricultural residues including sugarcane bagasse, cotton stalks, soybean husk, and pigeon pea waste into aviation-grade fuel using internationally certified conversion technologies, with phased commissioning planned through 2029.

The project reflects a broader corporate transition toward sustainability-driven growth and positions Maharashtra as an investment destination for transition-aligned industrial development. Sankla Buildcoon is applying its established capabilities in capital discipline, project execution, and stakeholder management to establish scalable clean energy platforms. The company views sustainable aviation fuel as a structurally growing market segment aligned with India's energy transition objectives.

title: Sankla Buildcoon Announces Fifteen Thousand Crore Rupee Sustainable Aviation Fuel Investment
summary:
Sankla Buildcoon has committed ₹15,000 crore to construct a Sustainable Aviation Fuel manufacturing facility in Maharashtra, with a formal memorandum of understanding signed at the World Economic Forum in Davos. The facility at Chalisgaon will process agricultural residues including sugarcane bagasse, cotton stalks, soybean husks, and pigeon pea waste into aviation-grade fuel using certified global technologies, with operations scheduled for phased commissioning through 2029.

The project is designed as a long-term infrastructure asset powered entirely by renewable energy, incorporating closed-loop water management and zero-liquid-discharge processes to meet global environmental standards. The initiative aims to support rural economies by establishing formal markets for agricultural waste, offering farmers long-term procurement contracts while reducing open-field burning, and is projected to generate over 3,000 direct and indirect jobs upon full commissioning.

title: US Government Initiates Formal Process For Possible California Offshore Oil Leasing
summary:
The Department of the Interior's Bureau of Ocean Energy Management has launched a public consultation process to assess potential offshore oil and gas leasing off the California coast. The move marks the first formal step toward possible lease auctions in federal waters off southern and central California, an area that has not seen new offshore oil leasing since the mid-1980s. The consultation period invites industry and public input on environmental conditions, social and economic impacts, and commercial interest in the proposed areas.

This initiative aligns with the Trump administration's energy dominance agenda, which seeks to increase domestic fossil fuel production and strengthen energy security. The action represents a significant policy shift given California's decades-long commitment to strict environmental regulations and climate goals, combined with strong public opposition to offshore drilling rooted in historical concerns including the 1969 Santa Barbara oil spill. Currently, California's offshore oil production contributes minimally to national output, with Pacific leases accounting for only 0.1 percent of federal waters production.

title: Trump Administration Invites Oil Industry Nominations for California Offshore Drilling Leases
summary:
The Trump administration's Interior Department has invited the oil and gas industry to nominate areas for potential offshore oil and gas lease sales in Southern and Central California, with auctions potentially beginning in 2027. This initiative represents an expansion of the administration's energy dominance agenda aimed at increasing U.S. fossil fuel production. The move marks the first time since 1984 that Pacific drilling rights have been considered for auction, breaking decades of protection following California's 1969 Santa Barbara oil spill.

California Governor Gavin Newsom and environmental groups have strongly opposed the plan, arguing it threatens coastal ecosystems and the state's economy. Pacific federal waters currently contribute only 0.1% of U.S. oil production despite accounting for 14% of total federal offshore output. The public and industry have 30 days to submit comments, with the Interior Department's Bureau of Ocean Energy Management overseeing the process.

title: Singapore's Keppel and Aster to Jointly Develop Sustainable Aviation Fuel Production Facility
summary:
Keppel and Aster have announced a joint partnership to assess the feasibility of constructing a sustainable aviation fuel production facility on Singapore's Jurong Island. The proposed plant would convert ethanol into SAF with an anticipated capacity of 100,000 metric tons annually. A feasibility study has already been completed, and the companies plan to proceed with front-end engineering design before making a final investment decision.

The project aligns with Singapore's regulatory push to reduce aviation sector emissions. The country has mandated the use of 1% SAF in aviation fuel starting this year and intends to increase the requirement to 3%-5% by 2030. This initiative positions Singapore as a strategic regional hub for low-carbon fuel production and addresses growing demand for sustainable alternatives in the aviation industry.

title: Keppel and Aster Partner on Singapore Sustainable Aviation Fuel Facility Development
summary:
Asset manager Keppel and energy solutions firm Aster have announced a collaboration to evaluate the development of an ethanol-to-jet sustainable aviation fuel facility on Jurong Island in Singapore. The proposed facility would produce up to 100,000 tonnes of SAF annually by converting bioethanol into jet fuel through chemical processes utilizing biomass and waste feedstocks. The project has received support from Singapore's Economic Development Board and Enterprise Singapore.

The partnership positions the facility as one of Asia's first purpose-built ethanol-to-jet SAF operations, aligned with Singapore's strategic goal to establish itself as a regional SAF hub. Both companies emphasized the facility's potential to address growing airline demand for sustainable aviation fuels while supporting decarbonization of the aviation sector across the region.
